info.title: Root API doc
info.description: The following document describes the REST API of Root API.

get.summary: get list of definitions from sub apps

201.description: A new credential has been created for the current user

400.description: Unsupported protocol, invalid or missing attribute

409.description: Conflict - a credential with this name already exist

post.summary: Create a new credential for the current user

get.summary: Get a credential, using the name as the unique identifier. The `name` is the string that will be inserted and displayed in the credential url. Example: smb://<NAME>@<server>/file

put.summary: Edit an existing credential, or create a new one if the provided `name` does not exist.

get.description:
The query string parameters for filtering (`status`, `index`, `id`, `name`, `priority`
`start_date`, `end_date`, `watchfolder_id`, `watchfolder_name`) restrict the items of the job list.
They can be mixed on the same request.
The query string parameter `sort` sorts the items of the filtered job list.
The query string parameters `offset` and `limit` restrict the items of the sortered job list.
The query string parameter 'fields' restricts the projection applied to the fields for each job.

schema.title: Object with the ID of the created resource

id.title: Job ID

post.summary: Convert a Job Configuration to the specified target version (latest by default). If no version key is provided in the Job Configuration, version 0.0.0 will be used.

requestBody.description: The job configuration to be updated

200.description: OK - Job configuration has been converted to target version

400.description: Bad Request - An error happened during job conversion

post.description:
Start multiple jobs. 
Jobs to start are specified by ID

requestBody.description: The list of job IDs to start

207.description:
Multi-jobs response. 
The response body contains an array of object, each object indicating the result of one job.

example.error: Start a pending job is not allowed

400.description: Bad Request response occuring when the request body does not respect the expected format

post.description:
Stop multiple jobs. 
Jobs to stop are specified by ID

requestBody.description: The list of job IDs to stop

207.description:
Multi-jobs response. 
The response body contains an array of object, each object indicating the result of one job.

example.error: Aborting an aborted job is not allowed

400.description: Bad Request response occuring when the request body does not respect the expected format

post.description:
Delete multiple jobs. 
Jobs to delete are specified by ID

requestBody.description: The list of job IDs to delete

207.description:
Multi-jobs response. 
The response body contains an array of object, each object indicating the result of one job.

example.error: Workflow can not be deleted while being running

400.description: Bad Request response occuring when the request body does not respect the expected format

get.description: Get the ESAM report for a job by providing its ID.

post.description: Used to set a job's priority, higher values mean a higher priority.

200.description: OK - Job priority has been set

get.description: Get a job's user data, given the job's ID.

200.description: OK - Job user data has been retrieved

post.description:
Used to set a job's user data, given the job's ID.
User data is limited to 1024 characters.

requestBody.description: The user data to be set

get.summary: Get a list of submitted probes
get.description:
The query string parameters for filtering (`state`, `creation_date`, `id`, `name`, `priority`
`start_date`, `end_date`) restrict the items of the probe list.
They can be mixed on the same request.
The query string parameter `sort` sorts the items of the filtered probe list.
The query string parameters `offset` and `limit` restrict the items of the sorted probe list.

schema.title: Object with the ID of the created resource

id.title: Probe ID

get.description: Get info for a specified probe request, using its id as the unique identifier. The `id` is the string returned when initially creating the probe

get.summary: Get state of a specific probe

get.description: Get all the existing Kantar server contexts

400.description: Unsupported protocol, invalid or missing attribute

409.description: Conflict - a Kantar server context with this name already exists

get.summary: Get a Kantar server by Name
get.description: Get a Kantar server, using its name as the unique identifier. The `name` is the same string used to refer to this context in a job

put.description: Edit an existing Kantar server context, or create a new one if the provided `name` does not exist.

delete.description: Delete an existing Kantar server context given its `name`

204.description: Kantar server context has been deleted

get.description:
Retrieve a list of templates.
The returned list can be sorted and filtered using the parametrs below.

post.description: Create a new template using the provided parameters.

schema.title: Object with the ID of the created resource

id.title: Template ID

get.description: Get a template specified by its unique identifier `id`.

delete.description:
This endpoint allows the deletion of a Template by providing its unique identifier `id`.
Please note that this action is irreversible.

schema.title: Watchfolder configuration array
schema.description: Array containing the configuration of all watchfolders

get.description:
Responds to requests with a basic 200 OK message.
Used for verifying that HTTP requests reach the API.

requestBody.description:
Database cleaner object.

The cleaner object holds a set of parameters that describe rules for cleaning the database.
The rules are executed upon a given 'period', and delete data according to their 'type' (jobs or alarms objects) and that are older than the 'resource_timeout'.

Multiple cleaner objects can be created and are identified by their UUID.

schema.title: Object with the ID of the created resource

id.title: Cleaner ID

delete.description: Execute parameter will be not used.

post.summary: Set terminated jobs expire time value

requestBody.description: The expiration time to be set

200.description: OK - Job expiration time has been set

400.description: Bad Request - An error happened during dbmanager request

400.description: Bad Request - An error happened dbmanager request

requestBody.description: The expiration time to be set

200.description: OK - Alarm expiration time has been set

400.description: Bad Request - An error happened during dbmanager request
Bad Request - An error happened dbmanager request

requestBody.description: The body must contains the full action to add.

schema.title: Action uuid
schema.description: This object is the uuid of the new action

id.title: Action ID

schema.title: Action
schema.description: This object is the actions corresponding to the id

requestBody.description:
Full updated action.

Since the action can by fully modified (add/remove parameters, change type)
the action must be complete and will replace all the previous parameters.

Missing parameters will be managed as removed parameters.

post.summary: Test an action for an event

204.description: No Content - Action test has ended as expected

400.description: Bad Request - Event not linked to the action

502.description: Proxy Error - Error from third party server

get.description:
Responds to requests with a basic 200 OK message.
Used for verifying that HTTP requests reach the API.

200.description: OK - Encoder Control has been created

limit.name: limit
limit.description: Maximum number of items that may be returned

offset.name: offset
offset.description: Offset of the first element to return

sort.name: sort
sort.description:
This parameter is a comma-separated list of sort keys.
You can append the sort direction to each sort key (separate with the ':' character).

The sort direction cab be either 'asc' for ascending order or 'desc' for descending order.

To sort with nested field, you must use the following dot notation: `field.nestedField`

with_details.name: with_details
with_details.description:
If this parameter is set to true, the return values of "fragmentation" and "playlist" fields will not be
compressed.

workflow_history.name: workflow_history
workflow_history.description:
If this parameter is set to true, all workflows are returned. Otherwise, only the last executed
workflow is returned.

resolve_and_update.name: resolve_and_update
resolve_and_update.description:
If this parameter is set to true, the returned configuration is resolved (templates) and then updated to the
latest version. Otherwise, the original configuration submitted is returned.

status.name: status
status.description:
A query string parameter that restricts the data returned from your request by filtering on Job statuses.
The format is a comma-separated list of strings.
The list can optionally be preceded by the NOT operator ('!') to specify inequality.

Examples:
- Job status is running:
`status=running`
- Job status is aborted OR completed:
`status=aborted,completed`
- Job status is NOT running:
`status=!running`
- Job status is NOT running AND NOT aborted:
`status=!running,aborted`

id.name: id
id.description:
A query string parameter that restricts the data returned from your request by filtering on Job IDs.
The format is a comma-separated list of string.
The list can optionally be preceded by the NOT operator ('!') to specify inequality.

Examples:
- Job ID is 9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5:
`id=9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5`
- Job ID is 9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5 OR b46edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5:
`id=9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5,b46edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5`
- Job ID is NOT 9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5:
`id=!9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5`
- Job ID is NOT 9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5 AND NOT b46edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5:
`id=!9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5,b46edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5`

index.name: index
index.description:
A query string parameter that restricts the data returned from your request by filtering on Job indexes.
The format is a comma-separated list whose elements have one of the following syntaxes:
- `INDEX` where INDEX is 0 or a positive integer.
This syntax represents a single index.
Examples:
  - Job index is 10:
  `index=10`
  - Job index is 45 OR 50:
  `index=45,50`
- `INDEX-START:` where INDEX-START is zero or a positive integer.
This syntax represents an index interval identified by start; a range
beginning at INDEX-START and ending at the largest index.
INDEX-START is included in the range.
Examples:
  - Job index is greater than or equal to 15:
  `index=15:`
  - Job index is greater than or equal to 3000:
  `index=3000:`
- `:INDEX-END` where INDEX-END is zero or a positive integer.
This syntax represents an index interval identified by end; a range
beginning at zero and ending at INDEX-END.
INDEX-END is included in the range.
Examples:
  - Job index is less than or equal to 20:
  `index=:20`
  - Job index is less than or equal to 299:
  `index=:299`
- `INDEX-START:INDEX-END:` where INDEX-START and INDEX-END are zero or positive integers.
This syntax represents an index interval identified by start and end; a range
beginning at INDEX-START and ending at INDEX-END.
INDEX-START and INDEX-END are included in the range.
Examples:
  - Job index is in the range starting at 15 and ending at 25:
  `index=15:20`
  - Job index is in the range starting at 15 and ending at 25 OR in the range
  starting at 72 and ending at 150:
  `index=15:20,72:150`

The various syntaxes may be mixed:
- Job index is 23 OR 30 OR less than or equal to 10:
`index=23,30,:10`
- Job index is 43 OR in the range starting at 12 and ending at 25:
`index=12:25,43`

name.name: name
name.description:
A query string parameter that restricts the data returned from your request by filtering on Job names.
The format is a comma-separated list of strings.
A match type operator can optionally be prepended to each value, separated by the colons (':') character.

| Operator    | Description                                 |
| ----------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| `eq`        | Exact match                                 |
| `neq`       | Does not match                              |
| `in`        | Contains substring                          |
| `nin`       | Does not contain any substrings             |
| `re`        | Contains a match for the regular expression |
| `nre`       | Does not match for the regular expression   |

Matching is always case-insensitive. If the operator is omitted,
it shall be assumed that the `in` operator apply.

Examples:
- Job name contains Job-HD:
`name=Job-HD`, `name=in:Job-HD`
- Job name does not contains Job-HD:
`name=nin:Job-HD`
- Job name is Job-HD:
`name=eq:Job-HD`
- Job name is not Job-HD:
`name=neq:Job-HD`
- Job name contains Job-HD OR Job-SD:
`name=Job-HD,Job-SD`, `name=re:Job-(HD|SD)`
- Job name is Job-OTT-1 OR Job-OTT-2 OR Job-OTT-3:
`name=re:^Job-OTT-[1-3]$`
- Job name starts with Job-O AND ends with a digit:
`name=re:^Job-O.*\d$`
- Job name does not contains digits:
`name=nre:\d`

priority.name: priority
priority.description:
A query string parameter that restricts the data returned from your request by filtering on Job priorities.
The format is a comma-separated list whose elements have one of the following syntaxes:
- `PRIORITY` where PRIORITY is 0 or a positive integer.
This syntax represents a single priority.
Examples:
  - Job priority is 10:
  `priority=10`
  - Job priority is 45 OR 50:
  `priority=45,50`
- `PRIORITY-START:` where PRIORITY-START is zero or a positive integer.
This syntax represents a priority interval identified by start; a range
beginning at PRIORITY-START and ending at the largest priority.
PRIORITY-START is included in the range.
Examples:
  - Job priority is greater than or equal to 15:
  `priority=15:`
  - Job priority is greater than or equal to 33:
  `priority=33:`
- `:PRIORITY-END` where PRIORITY-END is zero or a positive integer.
This syntax represents a priority interval identified by end; a range
beginning at zero and ending at PRIORITY-END.
PRIORITY-END is included in the range.
Examples:
  - Job priority is less than or equal to 20:
  `priority=:20`
  - Job priority is less than or equal to 60:
  `priority=:60`
- `PRIORITY-START:PRIORITY-END:` where PRIORITY-START and PRIORITY-END are zero or positive integers.
This syntax represents a priority interval identified by start and end; a range beginning at
PRIORITY-START and ending at PRIORITY-END.
PRIORITY-START and PRIORITY-END are included in the range.
Examples:
  - Job priority is in the range starting at 15 and ending at 20:
  `priority=15:20`
  - Job priority is in the range starting at 15 and ending at 20 OR in the range starting
  at 32 and ending at 77:
  `priority=15:20,32:77`

The various syntaxes may be mixed:
- Job priority is 23 OR 30 OR less than or equal to 10:
`priority=23,30,:10`
- Job priority is 43 OR in the range starting at 12 and ending at 25:
`priority=12:25,43`

start_date.name: start_date
start_date.description:
A query string parameter that restricts the data returned from your request by filtering on Job start dates.
The format is a comma-separated list whose elements have one of the following syntaxes:
- `TIME` where TIME is an *ISO-8601 datetime* string.
This syntax represents a single time identified by TIME.
If TIME is partial, an implicit time interval is assumed.
Examples:
  - Job start_date is equal on 1st August 2005 at 13 hours, 22 minutes and 18 seconds:
  `start_date=1991-09-25T13:22:18`
  - `start_date=2019-07-24T16` represents a time interval of one hour beginning on 24th July 2019 at 16 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds
  - `start_date=2019-07` represents a time interval of one month beginning on 1st July 2019 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds
- `TIME-START..` where TIME-START is an *ISO-8601 datetime* string.
This syntax represents a time interval identified by start;
a range beginning at TIME-START and ending at current time.
TIME-START is included in the range.
Examples:
  - Job start_date is greater than or equal on 1st August 2005 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds:
  `start_date=2005-08..`
  - Job start_date is greater than or equal on 12th April 1985 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds:
  `start_date=1985-04-12..`
  - Job start_date is greater than or equal on 25th September 1991 at 13 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds:
  `start_date=1991-09-25T13..`
- `..TIME-END` where TIME-END is an *ISO-8601 datetime* string.
This syntax represents a time interval identified by end;
a range beginning at Unix epoch (on 1st January 1970) and ending at TIME-END.
TIME-END is included in the range.
Examples:
  - Job start_date is less than or equal on 1st August 2005 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds:
  `start_date=..2005-08`
  - Job start_date is less than or equal on 12th April 1985 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds:
  `start_date=..1985-04-12`
  - Job start_date is less than or equal on 25th September 1991 at 13 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds:
  `start_date=..1991-09-25T13`
- `TIME-START/TIME-END` where TIME-START and TIME-END are *ISO-8601 datetime* strings.
This syntax represents a time interval identified by start and end;
a range beginning at TIME-START and ending at TIME-END.
TIME-START and TIME-END are included in the range.
Higher order time elements may be omitted from the expression following the solidus (i.e. the
representation for "end of time interval"); in such a case it shall be assumed that the
corresponding time elements from the "start of time interval" expression apply (e.g. if YYYY-MM
are omitted, the end of the time interval is in the same year and month as the start of the time interval).
Examples:
  - Job start_date is in the time interval beginning on 12th April 1985 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds
  and ending on 23rd June 1985 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds:
  `start_date=1985-04-12T00:00:00/1985-06-23T00:00:00`
  `start_date=1985-04-12/1985-06-23`
  `start_date=1985-04-12/06-23`
  - Job start_date is in the time interval beginning on 12th June 2009 at 16 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds
  and ending on 23rd July 2009 at 22 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds:
  `start_date=2009-06-12T16/2009-06-23T22`
  `start_date=2009-06-12T16/06-23T22`
  `start_date=2009-06-12T16/23T22`
- `DURATION/TIME-END` where TIME-END is an *ISO-8601 datetime* string and
DURATION an *ISO-8601 duration* string.
This syntax represents a time interval identified by duration and end;
a range of duration DURATION ending at TIME-END.
Examples:
  - Job start_date is in the time interval beginning on 10th April 1985 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds
  and ending on 12th April 1985 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds:
  `start_date=P2D/1985-04-12T00:00:00`
  `start_date=P2D/1985-04-12`
  - Job start_date is in the time interval beginning on 11th April 2017 at 12 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds
  and ending on 19th March 2018 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds:
  `start_date=P1Y6DT36H/2018-03-19T00:00:00`
  `start_date=P1Y6DT36H/2018-03-19`
- `TIME-START/DURATION` where TIME-START is an *ISO-8601 datetime* string and
DURATION an *ISO-8601 duration* string.
This syntax represents a time interval identified by start and duration;
a range of duration DURATION beginning at TIME-START.
Examples:
  - Job start_date is in the time interval beginning on 12th April 1985 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds
  and ending on 14th April 1985 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds:
  `start_date=1985-04-12T00:00:00/P2D`
  `start_date=1985-04-12/P2D`
  - Job end_date is in the time interval beginning on 19th March 2018 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds
  and ending on 27th March 2019 at 12 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds:
  `start_date=2018-03-19T00:00:00/P1Y6DT36H`
  `start_date=2018-03-19/P1Y6DT36H`

The various syntaxes may be mixed:
- Job start_date is less than or equal on 12th April 1985 at 17 hours, 34 minutes and 12 seconds OR
in the time interval beginning on 14th August 1985 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds
and ending on 18th August 1985 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds:
`start_date=..1985-04-12T17:34:12,1985-08-14/P4D`

An *ISO-8601 datetime* string consists of a time part or a date part, followed
optionally by a time part - the date and time parts are separated
by a single designator, which is `T` in the official
standard. Incomplete date formats (such as `YYYY-MM`) may *not* be
combined with a time part.
Supported formats are:
- `YYYY`
- `YYYY-MM`
- `YYYY-MM-DDThh`
- `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm`
- `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss`
- `Thh`       (implicit current date)
- `Thh:mm`    (implicit current date)
- `Thh:mm:ss` (implicit current date)

An *ISO-8601 duration* string consists of the designator `P` followed by a combination of components with designators.
The number of years shall be followed by the designator `Y`, the number of months by `M`,
the number of weeks by `W`, and the number of days by `D`. The part including time
components shall be preceded by the designator `T`; the number of hours shall be
followed by `H`, the number of minutes by `M` and the number of seconds by `S`.
The designator `T` shall be absent if all of the time components are absent.
If the number of years, months, days, hours, minutes or seconds in any of these expressions equals
zero, the number and the corresponding designator may be absent; however, at least one number
and its designator shall be present (`P5DT36H` is equivalent to `P0Y0M5DT36H0M0S`).

end_date.name: end_date
end_date.description:
A query string parameter that restricts the data returned from your request by filtering on Job end dates.
See [start_date](#start_date) parameter for a full description.

watchfolder_id.name: watchfolder_id
watchfolder_id.description:
A query string parameter that restricts the data returned from your request by filtering on Watchfolder IDs.
The format is a comma-separated list of string.
The list can optionally be preceded by the NOT operator ('!') to specify inequality.

ID example : ̀ b46edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5`

Examples:
- Watchfolder ID is <ID_A>:
`watchfolder_id=<ID_A>`
- Watchfolder ID is <ID_A> OR <ID_B>:
`watchfolder_id=<ID_A>,<ID_B>`
- Watchfolder ID is NOT <ID_A>:
`watchfolder_id=!<ID_A>`
- Watchfolder ID is NOT <ID_A> AND NOT <ID_B>:
`watchfolder_id=!<ID_A>,<ID_B>`

watchfolder_name.name: watchfolder_name
watchfolder_name.description:
A query string parameter that restricts the data returned from your request by filtering on Watchfolder names.
The format is a comma-separated list of strings.
A match type operator can optionally be prepended to each value, separated by the colons (':') character.

| Operator    | Description                                 |
| ----------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| `eq`        | Exact match                                 |
| `neq`       | Does not match                              |
| `in`        | Contains substring                          |
| `nin`       | Does not contain any substrings             |
| `re`        | Contains a match for the regular expression |
| `nre`       | Does not match for the regular expression   |

Matching is always case-insensitive. If the operator is omitted,
it shall be assumed that the `in` operator apply.

Examples:
- Watchfolder name contains Watchfolder-HD:
`watchfolder_name=Watchfolder-HD`, `watchfolder_name=in:Watchfolder-HD`
- Watchfolder name does not contains Watchfolder-HD:
`watchfolder_name=nin:Watchfolder-HD`
- Watchfolder name is Watchfolder-HD:
`watchfolder_name=eq:Watchfolder-HD`
- Watchfolder name is not Watchfolder-HD:
`watchfolder_name=neq:Watchfolder-HD`
- Watchfolder name contains Watchfolder-HD OR Watchfolder-SD:
`watchfolder_name=Watchfolder-HD,Watchfolder-SD`, `watchfolder_name=re:Watchfolder-(HD|SD)`
- Watchfolder name starts with Watchfolder- AND ends with a digit:
`watchfolder_name=re:^Watchfolder-.*\d$`
- Watchfolder name does not contains digits:
`watchfolder_name=nre:\d`

processing_queue.name: processing_queue
processing_queue.description:
A query string parameter that restricts the data returned from your request by filtering on processing queue.
The format is a comma-separated list of strings.
A match type operator can optionally be prepended to each value, separated by the colons (':') character.

| Operator    | Description                                 |
| ----------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| `eq`        | Exact match                                 |
| `neq`       | Does not match                              |
| `in`        | Contains substring                          |
| `nin`       | Does not contain any substrings             |
| `re`        | Contains a match for the regular expression |
| `nre`       | Does not match for the regular expression   |

Matching is always case-insensitive. If the operator is omitted,
it shall be assumed that the `in` operator apply.

Examples:
- Processing queue contains nodes-4k:
`processing_queue=nodes-4k`, `processing_queue=in:nodes-4k`
- Processing queue does not contains nodes-4k:
`processing_queue=nin:nodes-4k`
- Processing queue is nodes-4k:
`processing_queue=eq:nodes-4k`
- Processing queue is not nodes-4k:
`processing_queue=neq:nodes-4k`
- Processing queue contains nodes-4k OR nodes-hd:
`processing_queue=nodes-4k,nodes-hd`, `processing_queue=re:nodes-(4k|hd)`
- Processing queue is nodes-4k-1 OR nodes-4k-2 OR nodes-4k-3:
`processing_queue=re:^nodes-4k-[1-3]$`
- Processing queue starts with nodes-4k AND ends with a digit:
`processing_queue=re:^nodes-4k.*\d$`
- Processing queue does not contains digits:
`processing_queue=nre:\d`

fields.name: fields
fields.description:
A query string parameter that restricts the fields of each job returned in the response.\
The format is a comma-separated list of strings.
For nested fields, the list must be surrounded by `:(` and `)`.\
It acts as a projection.\
Each value must be a job field as described in the API.

* By default, projection is done on following fields: 
__name__, __id__, __priority__, __index__, __watchfolder_id__, __watchfolder_name__, __workflow_id__, __workflows__
* For each workflow in __workflows__, projection is done on:
__id__, __progress__, __steps__, __steps.tasks__, __status.state_machine.current__, 
__status.state_machine.reason__, __status.start_date__, __status.end_date__, __status.duration__, 
__status.active_duration__, __status.cpu_duration__, __status.processing_queue__

For nested fields in complex structures, here are some expressions to target them

| Expression                                          | Description                                                           |
| --------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `<parentField>:(<childField1>,...,<childFieldN>)`   | Targets some fields nested in an object or elements of an array       |

Examples:
- Get jobs with only the __name__ and __workflows__ fields:
`fields=name,workflows` 
- Get jobs with __name__ and, in all elements of __workflows__ list, __progress__ and __status.start_date__ fields:
`fields=name,workflows:(progress,status:(start_date))`

probe_id.name: id
probe_id.description: Probe ID, the same one returned at creation time.

limit.name: limit
limit.description: Maximum number of items that may be returned

offset.name: offset
offset.description: Offset of the first element to return

sort.name: sort
sort.description:
This parameter is a comma-separated list of sort keys. 
You can append the sort direction to each sort key (separate with the ':' character and having the following format: <sort_key>:<sort_direction>).

The sort direction cab be either 'asc' for ascending order or 'desc' for descending order.

To sort with nested field, you must use the following dot notation: `field.nestedField`

state.name: state
state.description:
A query string parameter that restricts the data returned from your request by filtering on Probe statuses.
The format is a comma-separated list of strings.
The list can optionally be preceded by the NOT operator ('!') to specify inequality.

Examples:
- Probe status is running:
`state=running`
- Probe status is aborted OR completed:
`state=aborted,completed`
- Probe status is NOT running:
`state=!running`
- Probe status is NOT running AND NOT aborted:
`state=!running,aborted`

id.name: id
id.description:
A query string parameter that restricts the data returned from your request by filtering on Probe IDs.
The format is a comma-separated list of string.
The list can optionally be preceded by the NOT operator ('!') to specify inequality.

Examples:
- Probe ID is 9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5:
`id=9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5`
- Probe ID is 9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5 OR b46edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5:
`id=9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5,b46edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5`
- Probe ID is NOT 9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5:
`id=!9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5`
- Probe ID is NOT 9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5 AND NOT b46edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5:
`id=!9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5,b46edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5`

name.name: name
name.description:
A query string parameter that restricts the data returned from your request by filtering on Probe names.
The format is comma-separated list of strings.
A match type operator can optionally be prepended to each value, separated by the colons (':') character.

| Operator    | Description                                 |
| ----------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| `eq`        | Exact match                                 |
| `neq`       | Does not match                              |
| `in`        | Contains substring                          |
| `nin`       | Does not contains substring                 |
| `re`        | Contains a match for the regular expression |
| `nre`       | Does not match for the regular expression   |

Matching is always case-insensitive. If the operator is omitted,
it shall be assumed that the `in` operator apply.

Examples:
- Probe name contains Probe-HD:
`name=Probe-HD`, `name=in:Probe-HD`
- Probe name does not contains Probe-HD:
`name=nin:Probe-HD`
- Probe name is Probe-HD:
`name=eq:Probe-HD`
- Probe name is not Probe-HD:
`name=neq:Probe-HD`
- Probe name contains Probe-HD OR Probe-SD:
`name=Probe-HD,Probe-SD`, `name=re:Probe-(HD|SD)`
- Probe name is Probe-OTT-1 OR Probe-OTT-2 OR Probe-OTT-3:
`name=re:^Probe-OTT-[1-3]$`
- Probe name starts with Probe-O AND ends with a digit:
`name=re:^Probe-O.*\d$`
- Probe name does not contains digits:
`name=nre:\d`

priority.name: priority
priority.description:
A query string parameter that restricts the data returned from your request by filtering on Probe priorities.
The format is a comma-separated list whose elements have one of the following syntaxes:
- `PRIORITY` where PRIORITY is 0 or a positive integer.
This syntax represents a single priority.
Examples:
  - Probe priority is 10:
  `priority=10`
  - Probe priority is 45 OR 50:
  `priority=45,50`
- `PRIORITY-START:` where PRIORITY-START is zero or a positive integer.
This syntax represents a priority interval identified by start; a range
beginning at PRIORITY-START and ending at the largest priority.
PRIORITY-START is included in the range.
Examples:
  - Probe priority is greater than or equal to 15:
  `priority=15:`
  - Probe priority is greater than or equal to 33:
  `priority=33:`
- `:PRIORITY-END` where PRIORITY-END is zero or a positive integer.
This syntax represents a priority interval identified by end; a range
beginning at zero and ending at PRIORITY-END.
PRIORITY-END is included in the range.
Examples:
  - Probe priority is less than or equal to 20:
  `priority=:20`
  - Probe priority is less than or equal to 60:
  `priority=:60`
- `PRIORITY-START:PRIORITY-END:` where PRIORITY-START and PRIORITY-END are zero or positive integers.
This syntax represents a priority interval identified by start and end; a range beginning at
PRIORITY-START and ending at PRIORITY-END.
PRIORITY-START and PRIORITY-END are included in the range.
Examples:
  - Probe priority is in the range starting at 15 and ending at 20:
  `priority=15:20`
  - Probe priority is in the range starting at 15 and ending at 20 OR in the range starting
  at 32 and ending at 77:
  `priority=15:20,32:77`

The various syntaxes may be mixed:
- Probe priority is 23 OR 30 OR less than or equal to 10:
`priority=23,30,:10`
- Probe priority is 43 OR in the range starting at 12 and ending at 25:
`priority=12:25,43`

start_date.name: start_date
start_date.description: A query string parameter that restricts the data returned from your request by filtering on Probe start dates. The format is a comma-separated list whose elements have one of the following syntaxes: - `TIME` where TIME is an [*ISO-8601 datetime*](#iso_datetime) string. This syntax represents a single time identified by TIME. If TIME is partial, an implicit time interval is assumed. Examples: - Probe start_date is equal on 1st August 2005 at 13 hours, 22 minutes and 18 seconds: `start_date=2005-08-01T13:22:18` - `start_date=2019-07-24T16` represents a time interval of one hour beginning on 24th July 2019 at 16 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds - `start_date=2019-07` represents a time interval of one month beginning on 1st July 2019 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds - `TIME-START..` where TIME-START is an [*ISO-8601 datetime*](#iso_datetime) string. This syntax represents a time interval identified by start; a range beginning at TIME-START and ending at current time. TIME-START is included in the range. Examples: - Probe start_date is greater than or equal on 1st August 2005 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds: `start_date=2005-08..` - Probe start_date is greater than or equal on 12th April 1985 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds: `start_date=1985-04-12..` - Probe start_date is greater than or equal on 25th September 1991 at 13 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds: `start_date=1991-09-25T13..` - `..TIME-END` where TIME-END is an [*ISO-8601 datetime*](#iso_datetime) string. This syntax represents a time interval identified by end; a range beginning at Unix epoch (on 1st January 1970) and ending at TIME-END. TIME-END is included in the range. Examples: - Probe start_date is less than or equal on 1st August 2005 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds: `start_date=..2005-08` - Probe start_date is less than or equal on 12th April 1985 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds: `start_date=..1985-04-12` - Probe start_date is less than or equal on 25th September 1991 at 13 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds: `start_date=..1991-09-25T13` - `TIME-START/TIME-END` where TIME-START and TIME-END are [*ISO-8601 datetime*](#iso_datetime) strings. This syntax represents a time interval identified by start and end; a range beginning at TIME-START and ending at TIME-END. TIME-START and TIME-END are included in the range. Higher order time elements may be omitted from the expression following the solidus (i.e. the representation for "end of time interval"); in such a case it shall be assumed that the corresponding time elements from the "start of time interval" expression apply (e.g. if YYYY-MM are omitted, the end of the time interval is in the same year and month as the start of the time interval). Examples: - Probe start_date is in the time interval beginning on 12th April 1985 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds and ending on 23rd June 1985 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds: `start_date=1985-04-12T00:00:00/1985-06-23T00:00:00` `start_date=1985-04-12/1985-06-23` `start_date=1985-04-12/06-23` - Probe start_date is in the time interval beginning on 12th June 2009 at 16 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds and ending on 23rd July 2009 at 22 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds: `start_date=2009-06-12T16/2009-06-23T22` `start_date=2009-06-12T16/06-23T22` `start_date=2009-06-12T16/23T22` - `DURATION/TIME-END` where TIME-END is an [*ISO-8601 datetime*](#iso_datetime) string and DURATION an [*ISO-8601 duration*](#iso_duration) string. This syntax represents a time interval identified by duration and end; a range of duration DURATION ending at TIME-END. Examples: - Probe start_date is in the time interval beginning on 10th April 1985 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds and ending on 12th April 1985 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds: `start_date=P2D/1985-04-12T00:00:00` `start_date=P2D/1985-04-12` - Probe start_date is in the time interval beginning on 11th April 2017 at 12 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds and ending on 19th March 2018 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds: `start_date=P1Y6DT36H/2018-03-19T00:00:00` `start_date=P1Y6DT36H/2018-03-19` - `TIME-START/DURATION` where TIME-START is an [*ISO-8601 datetime*](#iso_datetime) string and DURATION an [*ISO-8601 duration*](#iso_duration) string. This syntax represents a time interval identified by start and duration; a range of duration DURATION beginning at TIME-START. Examples: - Probe start_date is in the time interval beginning on 12th April 1985 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds and ending on 14th April 1985 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds: `start_date=1985-04-12T00:00:00/P2D` `start_date=1985-04-12/P2D` - Probe end_date is in the time interval beginning on 19th March 2018 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds and ending on 27th March 2019 at 12 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds: `start_date=2018-03-19T00:00:00/P1Y6DT36H` `start_date=2018-03-19/P1Y6DT36H` The various syntaxes may be mixed: - Probe start_date is less than or equal on 12th April 1985 at 17 hours, 34 minutes and 12 seconds OR in the time interval beginning on 14th August 1985 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds and ending on 18th August 1985 at 0 hours, 0 minutes and 0 seconds: `start_date=..1985-04-12T17:34:12,1985-08-14/P4D` An *ISO-8601 datetime* string consists of a time part or a date part, followed optionally by a time part - the date and time parts are separated by a single designator, which is `T` in the official standard. Incomplete date formats (such as `YYYY-MM`) may *not* be combined with a time part. Supported formats are: - `YYYY` - `YYYY-MM` - `YYYY-MM-DDThh` - `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm` - `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss` - `Thh` (implicit current date) - `Thh:mm` (implicit current date) - `Thh:mm:ss` (implicit current date) An *ISO-8601 duration* string consists of the designator `P` followed by a combination of components with designators. The number of years shall be followed by the designator `Y`, the number of months by `M`, the number of weeks by `W`, and the number of days by `D`. The part including time components shall be preceded by the designator `T`; the number of hours shall be followed by `H`, the number of minutes by `M` and the number of seconds by `S`. The designator `T` shall be absent if all of the time components are absent. If the number of years, months, days, hours, minutes or seconds in any of these expressions equals zero, the number and the corresponding designator may be absent; however, at least one number and its designator shall be present (`P5DT36H` is equivalent to `P0Y0M5DT36H0M0S`).

end_date.name: end_date
end_date.description:
A query string parameter that restricts the data returned from your request by filtering on Probe end dates.

creation_date.name: creation_date

strict_resolution.name: strict_resolution
strict_resolution.description:
Determines whether to enforce strict resolution when referencing templates.
If set to `true`, an error response will be returned if a referenced template cannot be found during resolution.

limit.name: limit
limit.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned.

offset.name: offset

sort.name: sort
sort.description:
This parameter is a comma-separated list of sort keys.
You can append the sort direction to each sort key (separate with the ':' character).

The sort direction can be either `asc` for ascending order or `desc` for descending order.

To sort with nested field, you must use the following dot notation: `field.nestedField`

id.name: id
id.description: Watchfolder id

sort.name: sort
sort.description:
This parameter is a comma-separated list of sort keys. 
You can append the sort direction to each sort key (separate with the ':' character).
The sort direction can be either 'asc' for ascending order or 'desc' for descending order.
Supported sort keys are: Name, UID, InputFolderUri, OutputFolderUri, OutputFilePath and Filter.
Examples:
- Sort by ascending name:
`sort=Name`, `sort=Name:asc`
- Sort by descending name:
`sort=Name:desc`
- Sort by descending input folder uri and ascending name:
`sort=InputFolderUri:desc,Name:asc,`

name.name: Name
name.description:
A query string parameter that restricts the data returned from your request by filtering on Watchfolder names.
The format is a comma-separated list of strings.
A match type operator can optionally be prepended to each value, separated by the colons (':') character.

| Operator    | Description                                 |
| ----------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| `eq`        | Exact match                                 |
| `neq`       | Does not match                              |
| `in`        | Contains substring                          |
| `nin`       | Does not contain any substrings             |
| `re`        | Contains a match for the regular expression |
| `nre`       | Does not match for the regular expression   |

Matching is always case-insensitive. If the operator is omitted,
it shall be assumed that the `in` operator apply.

Examples:
- Watchfolder name contains Watchfolder-HD:
`Name=Watchfolder-HD`, `Name=in:Watchfolder-HD`
- Watchfolder name does not contains Watchfolder-HD:
`Name=nin:Watchfolder-HD`
- Watchfolder name is Watchfolder-HD:
`Name=eq:Watchfolder-HD`
- Watchfolder name is not Watchfolder-HD:
`Name=neq:Watchfolder-HD`
- Watchfolder name contains Watchfolder-HD OR Watchfolder-SD:
`Name=Watchfolder-HD,Watchfolder-SD`, `Name=re:Watchfolder-(HD|SD)`
- Watchfolder name is Watchfolder-OTT-1 OR Watchfolder-OTT-2 OR Watchfolder-OTT-3:
`Name=re:^Watchfolder-OTT-[1-3]$`
- Watchfolder name starts with Watchfolder-O AND ends with a digit:
`Name=re:^Watchfolder-O.*\d$`
- Watchfolder name does not contains digits:
`Name=nre:\d`

uid.name: UID
uid.description:

The format is a comma-separated list of string.
The list can optionally be preceded by the NOT operator ('!') to specify inequality.

Examples:

- Watchfolder ID is 9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5 OR b46edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5:

- Watchfolder ID is NOT 9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5:
`UID=!9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5`
- Watchfolder ID is NOT 9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5 AND NOT b46edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5:
`UID=!9e6edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5,b46edd56-3f6f-11eb-8692-cea19fdb40f5`

Comma-separated list of events name.

This list will be use to find all linked actions of these events.

Should seen as OR: ?events=e1,e2 for e1 or e2

ftp_yaml.description: Provide necessary options and credentials for an FTP storage if not already provided in the URL.

s3-role_yaml.description: Provides necessary options and credentials for an Amazon Simple Storage Service storage using a IAM role.

session_token_timeout.description: The duration of the token validity period in seconds in the range [3600..43200]

ca_bundle.title: Certificate Authority Bundle
ca_bundle.description: Specifies the base64-encoded CA bundle (certificate authority in PEM format) to use when verifying SSL certificates. If your CA is different of the default trust store, you can tell AWS S3 clients to use your own CA instead. You can use the linux base64 command to get the base64 chain from your file certificate (base64 -w 0 CA_BUNDLE.crt).

gcs_yaml.title: Credentials and options for GCS services
gcs_yaml.description: Provide necessary options and credentials for a Google Cloud storage.

id.title: Job ID

name.title: Job Name
name.description: The user friendly name of the job.

index.title: Job Index
index.description:
This value represent the position of the job in the processing queue.
If multiple jobs have the same priority, the  job with the lower index
will be processed first.

To change the processing order of the jobs, the priority filed must be used.

This value is never reset, even if other jobs created before have been deleted.

priority.title: Job Priority
priority.description:
The priority of the job determines the processing order of this job.
Higher priority jobs will be processed first.

workflow_id.title: Workflow ID
workflow_id.description: The ID of the current processing workflow.

workflows.title: Workflows List
workflows.description:
This array contains the list of workflows created by the job and
represents the job execution history. It is sorted by execution time
in descending order (i.e. the first entry is the most recently executed workflow).

configuration.title: JSON Job Configuration
configuration.description:
On latest versions, `JSON Job Configuration` will be used.

Older versions will use `JSON Job Configuration (older versions)` and attempt a conversion to the latest version.

uri.title: Asset URL
uri.description: URL of the asset in an internet-like format such as : protocol://credential@directory/filename.ext

type.title: Asset type
type.description: This field is used to differentiate the asset type when it is not obvious from the file extension

audio_composition.title: Audio composition
audio_composition.description:
With the audio composition, you can group multiple input audio tracks into multi-channel audio tracks.
The multi-channel audio tracks consist of an array of channels number.
This is limited to mono-channel or dual mono-channel PCM inputs embedded in a container such as MXF or MOV.
A classic usecase would be to aggregate an AES3 pair from 2 PCM mono-channel tracks.

optional.title: Asset optional
optional.description:
If set to true, this field allows the job to be transcoded even if the asset does not exist or it is inaccessible. It is assumed 
that no input with mandatory mapping policy uses this asset.

oneOf.title: Asset URL
oneOf.description: Deprecated since TF 3.0. Will be removed in a future release.

timecode_synchronization.description:
When this option is enabled, video and subtitles tracks will be synchronized based on their respective timecodes. 
This synchronization applies to STL, SCC, MCC and PAC files. 

- If enabled: Subtitles will start at the position that matches the video's first timecode.
- If disabled: Subtitles are not synchronized with video timecodes, and will be displayed according to their own timecodes.

Special case for STL files: Subtitles will start at the position that matches the "Start-of-Program" field in the STL file.

Examples:
- Enabled: If a video starts at 00:00:10 with two subtitles entries at 00:00:05 and at 00:00:10, only the 00:00:10 subtitle will be displayed.
- Disabled: The first subtitle will be displayed at 00:00:05 regardless of the video's timecodes.

trimmings.description:
Time range of the inputs that will be transcoded for this segment. The start time is inclusive, and the end time 
is exclusive, producing a [start,end[ interval for an easier concatenation configuration ([T1,T2[ + [T2,T3[ will 
not contain duplicate frames).

esam.title: ESAM configuration
esam.description:
You can use the ESAM configuration to specify the source of ESAM _SignalNotificationProcessing_ messages from 
a CableLabs XML file or from the provided content. 
The application produces a XML report, listing processed elements. 
This report is returned in the job statistics section. 
If the input frame rate is different from 30000/1001, you must use the frame rate parameter to specify the input frame rate.

source.title: Source
source.description: Source, asset or XML string, that contains ESAM _SignalNotificationProcessing_ messages. You must provide only an asset or an XML string.

asset.title: Source asset
asset.description: Source asset for the CableLabs XML file that contains ESAM _SignalNotificationProcessing_ messages.

xml.title: XML Content
xml.description: XML content of a CableLabs XML that contains ESAM _SignalNotificationProcessing__ messages.

frame_rate.title: Frame Rate
frame_rate.description:
Reference frame rate used to round the dates of the ESAM message to the nearest video frame. 
This frame rate is a rational number expressed by a list where the first element is the numerator and the 
second element is the denominator.

scte35_pid.title: SCTE35 PID
scte35_pid.description: SCTE35 PID, where 'auto' is replaced by a suitable default value.

idr_chaptering.title: IDR chaptering configuration
idr_chaptering.description:
Enforces IDR (Instantaneous Decoder Refresh) frames at the specified times. Cannot be used along with a regular chunking.
Times are relative to the input timeline: they don't need to be modified when using trimming or framerate override.
Duplicates and values outside of the segment's timeline will be ignored.

_________common_definitions_track_yaml.title: Track description
_________common_definitions_track_yaml.description:
A track associates one or more input tracks with an encoding configuration

Generic track-wide settings, such as language or also the mapping policy to use when the input is missing,
can also be configured in the overrides of the input object.

Multiple input tracks is not allowed for all kinds of tracks.
It can only be specified for audio tracks to support aggregation of pcm input streams.

input.title: Track input description
input.description:
A track input designates an input, and additional parameters on how to handle
the output track depending on the input.

3d_secondary_id.description:
Reference to the ID of the input containing a second video input for stereoscopic 3D.
The referenced input is expected to be for the right eye, with the main input covering the left view.
It can target the same value as the "id" used for the main input. This behavior can be used when the
same input has both views (in a top-bottom or side-by-side fashion) which can be separated with cropping.
This field is only used for multiview HEVC encodings.

mapping_policy.description:
Behavior if no actual input can be mapped.
- mandatory: the entire job will fail.
- optional: the track mapped to the input will be removed and the job will be transcoded.

input.title: Track input description
input.description:
A track input designates an input, and additional parameters on how to handle
the output track depending on the input.
Following modes are supported:
- normal audio track: only __id__ is set
- broadcast mix audio description: both __id__ and __audio_description_id__ are set. 
  The resulting audio track is a mix of a main content and a description content.
  In this mode, the signaling in TS outputs will be automatically done inside the supplementary_audio 
  descriptor.
  Notes:
  - if you want the signaling to be compliant with legacy receivers (not able to parse the 
    supplementary_audio descriptor), in the _overrides_ section, you must configure the language to "qad" or 
    "NAR". The right language will be set in the supplementary_audio descriptor.
  - you can customize the TS signaling in the _signaling_ section of the track configuration

Receiver mix audio description mode is not supported.

audio_description_id.description:
Reference to the ID of the input containing an audio description content.
The referenced input must be mono (voice) or stereo (voice + metadata)

mapping_policy.description:
Behavior if no actual input can be mapped.
- mandatory: the entire job will fail.
- optional: the track mapped to the input will be removed and the job will be transcoded.
- silence: the input will be replaced by silence and the job will be transcoded.

inputs.title: Track input list
inputs.description: A list of inputs used in this track. Multiple inputs can only be used for multi-page teletext.

items.title: Track input description
items.description:
A track input designates an input, and additional parameters on how to handle
the output track depending on the input.

mapping_policy.description:
Behavior if no actual input can be mapped.
- mandatory: the entire job will fail.
- optional: the track mapped to the input will be removed and the job will be transcoded.

input.title: Track input description
input.description:
A track input designates an input, and additional parameters on how to handle
the output track depending on the input.

mapping_policy.description:
Behavior if no actual input can be mapped.
- mandatory: the entire job will fail.
- optional: the track mapped to the input will be removed and the job will be transcoded.

_________common_definitions_track_video_.title: Video Track Overrides
_________common_definitions_track_video_.description:
Output track metadata are taken from input by default. Those values can be overriden with the following parameters.
The values here are not used for interpreting the input signal during processing (unlike values overridable at input level),
which allows each output track to have its own overrides.

_________common_definitions_track_audio_.title: Audio Track Overrides
_________common_definitions_track_audio_.description:
Output track metadata are taken from input by default. Those values can be overriden with the following parameters.
The values here are not used for interpreting the input signal during processing (unlike values overridable at input level),
which allows each output track to have its own overrides.

_________common_definitions_track_overri.title: Language Override
_________common_definitions_track_overri.description:
Overrides language, as an ISO 639 two-letter/three-letter code or a full name.
Non-ISO 639 are also allowed only with two-letter/three-letter code

_________common_definitions_track_subtit.title: Subtitles Track Overrides
_________common_definitions_track_subtit.description:
Output track metadata are taken from input by default. Those values can be overriden with the following parameters.
The values here are not used for interpreting the input signal during processing (unlike values overridable at input level),
which allows each output track to have its own overrides.

from.title: Output configuration to use as parent.

profile.title: Profile
profile.description: Fragmented MP4 profile.

subtitles_encapsulation.title: Subtitle Encapsulation
subtitles_encapsulation.description: Specifies audio encapsulation type: Raw or embedded in a MP4 container

enable_video_codec_switching.title: Enable Video Codec Switching
enable_video_codec_switching.description: Specifies whether different video codecs can be in the same adaptation set in the playlist or not.

enable_audio_codec_switching.title: Enable Audio Codec Switching
enable_audio_codec_switching.description: Specifies whether different audio codecs can be in the same adaptation set in the playlist or not.

preferential_language_code.title: Preferential Language Code
preferential_language_code.description:
Specifies which language code should be used preferentially in the manifest.
Passthrough will reuse the value specified in the track (from track settings, or from input language)

timescale.title: Default Muxer Timescale
timescale.description: Defines the default muxer timescale, which will be applied to all tracks whose specific timescale is not specified. If absent, it is automatically set from the video frame rate, from the audio sample rate or to a constant default value.

id.title: ID(s) of the track(s) to use
id.description:
This ID can be a simple string or an object. The object allow to associate secondary track to the first one.
For now its only used to add a closed caption track to a video track.

adaptation_set_id.title: Explicit Adaptation Set ID of the output track.
adaptation_set_id.description:
Sets the ID of the custom adaptation set the track should belong to, resulting in all compatible
tracks (same type, language, role, accessibility and codec if codec switching is disabled)
having the same `adaptation_set_id` will be grouped together and considered as representations
of the same adaptation set.
Note that by default (field missing), only compatible tracks coming from the same input are considered as
representations of the same adaptation set.
This field should be used to override the default behavior by allowing compatible tracks coming from different
inputs to be grouped together in the same adaptation set (example: multiple audio pass-through tracks representing
the same content at various quality level).
This is irrelevant for video, where all tracks (regardless of their origins) are considered
representations of a single adaptation set (no multi-angle support).

representation_id.title: Representation ID
representation_id.description:
Representation ID of output track in the playlist. If this field is missing, the track ID will be used to
present the track in the playlist.

adaptation_set_labels.title: Adaptation Set Labels
adaptation_set_labels.description:
A list of label elements (according to §5.3.10 Label and Group Label of MPEG DASH ISO 23009-1 standard).
These labels will be inserted in the adaptation set using this media.
When multiple media end up under the same adaptation set, only the first non-empty label list will be used.

id.title: Label identifier

media_path.title: Media Path
media_path.description:
Sets the path of the stream fragments (see Output File Paths). File extension should be set according to the stream type:
  * video: .mp4
  * audio: .mp4
  * data (WebVTT subtitles): .vtt, .mp4
Following wildcards can be used to define the media path template:
  * ${OUTPUT_BASE_URL}: Full path to the output folder of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${OUTPUT_FILE_NAME}: Filename without extension of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${TRACK_NAME}: Name of the track currently configured
  * ${EXT}: Extension automatically defined according to media type and subtitles_encapsulation
Following wildcards are only useful in live profile:
  * ${CHUNK_NUMBER}: Segment index
  * ${TIME}: Segment time, in timescale units.

initialization_segment_path.title: Initialization Segment Path
initialization_segment_path.description:
Sets the initialization segment path for the associated stream. This section is only relevant with the
following configuration:
  * live profile
additional wildcards can be used to define initialization segment path template (cf media path)

timescale.title: Specific Track Timescale
timescale.description: Defines a specific timescale for the track. If absent, the default muxer timescale (specified in the global muxer settings) will be used.

from.title: Output configuration to use as parent.

id.title: ID of the track to use

from.title: Output configuration to use as parent.

independent_media_storage.title: Independent Media Storage
independent_media_storage.description:
Specifies whether each generated stream must be output as a single file or if video and audios must be muxed
together.

media_segment_format.title: Media Segment Format
media_segment_format.description:
Sets the format of the media segments. The following formats are supported:
  * __ts__: generates MPEG-2 Transport Stream media segments.
  * __fmp4__: generates fragmented MP4 media segments.

audio_encapsulation.title: Audio Encapsulation
audio_encapsulation.description:
Specifies audio encapsulation type: Raw or embedded in a MPEG-2 Transport Stream container if
__Independent Media Storage__ is Enabled

use_byte_range.title: Use Byte Range
use_byte_range.description:
Specifies whether the EXT-X-BYTERANGE tag may be used or not. If enabled, a single file will be generated,
instead of many chunks.

initial_pts.title: Initial PTS
initial_pts.description: Specifies the PTS value in seconds for the content start time when the _Media Segment Format_ is ts.

chunks_boundaries_only.title: Chunk's initial iframes only
chunks_boundaries_only.description:
When false, all I frames are listed.
When true, only the first I frame for each chunk are listed.
This setting is only used for HLS TS. For HLS fMP4, the only supported behavior is to list the first I frame for each chunk.

playlist_path.title: Iframes only playlist path
playlist_path.description:
Path where to write the playlist.
Following wildcards can be used to define the media path template:
  * ${OUTPUT_BASE_URL}: Full path to the output folder of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${OUTPUT_FILE_NAME}: Filename without extension of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${PROFILE_NAME}: Name of the profile from which iframes are extracted
  * ${TRACK_NAME}: Name of the video track in the profile from which iframes are extracted
  * ${EXT}: Extension of the URL defined in __output__

id.title: ID(s) of the track(s) to use
id.description:
This ID can be a simple string or an object. The object allow to associate secondary track to the first one.
For now its only used to add a closed caption track to a video track.

playlist_path.title: Playlist Path
playlist_path.description:
Sets the path of the stream playlist, i.e. the playlist listing all the fragments of this stream
(see Output File Paths). For all streams when Independent Media Storage is enabled.
File extension should be .m3u8.
Following wildcards can be used to define the media path template:
  * ${OUTPUT_BASE_URL}: Full path to the output folder of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${OUTPUT_FILE_NAME}: Filename without extension of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${TRACK_NAME}: Name of the track currently configured
  * ${EXT}: Extension of the URL defined in __output__

media_path.title: Media Path
media_path.description:
Sets the path of the stream fragments (see Output File Paths). It is used for all streams when
Independent Media Storage is enabled.
Fragment number will be automatically appended to the radix of the specified filename.
File extension should be set according to the stream type:
  * video: .ts, .mp4
  * audio: .aac
Following wildcards can be used to define the media path template:
  * ${OUTPUT_BASE_URL}: Full path to the output folder of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${OUTPUT_FILE_NAME}: Filename without extension of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${TRACK_NAME}: Name of the track currently configured
  * ${EXT}: Extension automatically defined according to stream type, media_segment_format and audio_encapsulation
Following wildcards are only useful in live profile:
  * ${CHUNK_NUMBER}: Segment index
  * ${TIME}: Segment time, in timescale units.

still_image_path.title: Still Image Path
still_image_path.description:
Sets the path of an input image that will be embedded as an APIC ID3 tag in the stream. This is relevant
only for audio streams. A still image may be defined for each stream of an audio-only profile used as a
low-bitrate profile to replace the video with a message indicating that the bandwidth is too low to play
the content.

initialization_segment_path.title: Initialization Segment Path
initialization_segment_path.description:
Sets the initialization segment path for the associated stream. This section is only relevant with the
following configuration:
  * fMP4 media segment format
  * Byte-Range disabled
  * Video or audio stream.
Following wildcards can be used to define the media path template:
  * ${OUTPUT_BASE_URL}: Full path to the output folder of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${OUTPUT_FILE_NAME}: Filename without extension of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${TRACK_NAME}: Name of the track currently configured
  * ${EXT}: Extension automatically defined according to stream type and audio_encapsulation

drms.description: list of DRMs names, declared in `drms` to include to the track

profiles.title: Profiles description
profiles.description: Specify the profiles that will appear in the master playlist. This section is only used when independent media storage is not activated.

playlist_path.title: Playlist Path
playlist_path.description:
Sets the path of the profile playlist, i.e. the playlist listing all the media fragments of the
profile (see Output File Paths). It is used only when Independent Media Storage is disabled. File
extension should be .m3u8.
Following wildcards can be used to define the media path template:
  * ${OUTPUT_BASE_URL}: Full path to the output folder of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${OUTPUT_FILE_NAME}: Filename without extension of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${PROFILE_NAME}: name of the profile currently configured
  * ${EXT}: Extension of the URL defined in __output__

media_path.title: Media Path
media_path.description:
Sets the path of the profile fragments (see Output File Paths). It is used only when
Independent Media Storage is disabled. Fragment number will be automatically appended to the radix of
the specified filename. File extension should be .ts for a MPEG-2 TS fragment or .aac for a fragment
of an audio-only profile.
Following wildcards can be used to define the media path template:
  * ${OUTPUT_BASE_URL}: Full path to the output folder of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${OUTPUT_FILE_NAME}: Filename without extension of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${PROFILE_NAME}: Name of the profile currently configured
  * ${EXT}: ".ts" (only media_segment_format supported with disabled Independent Media Storage)
Following wildcards are only useful in live profile:
  * ${CHUNK_NUMBER}: Segment index
  * ${TIMESCALE}: Segment timescale value.

items.title: List of Muxer Tracks
items.description: The list of the Muxer tracks ID that must be used to create this profile.
ID of a profile to use

master_playlist_path.title: Playlist path
master_playlist_path.description:
Sets the path of the master playlist. This master playlist will point to the media playlist matching with
the profiles listed in the presentation.
Following wildcards can be used to define the media path template:
  * ${OUTPUT_BASE_URL}: Full path to the output folder of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${OUTPUT_FILE_NAME}: Filename without extension of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${PRESENTATION_NAME}: name of the presentation currently configured
  * ${EXT}: Extension of the URL defined in __output__

server.description: uses server parameters to get DRM data from a key server.

server_context.title: Server Context
server_context.description: Name of the KMS server context, as it has been stored on the KMS server entrypoint.

system_id.description:
Protection system identifier. Unknown system identifiers are accepted, but mechanics specific to them may not be implemented.
Following system identifiers can be set and the corresponding ID will be used:
  * fairplay: 94ce86fb-07ff-4f43-adb8-93d2fa968ca2
  * vcas: 9a27dd82-fde2-4725-8cbc-4234aa06ec09
  * widevine: edef8ba9-79d6-4ace-a3c8-27dcd51d21ed
  * playready: 9a04f079-9840-4286-ab92-e65be0885f95

content_id.description: Identifier that will be injected in the media.

from.title: Output configuration to use as parent.

id.title: ID(s) of the track(s) to use
id.description:
This ID can be a simple string or an object. The object allow to associate secondary track to the first one.
For now its only used to add a closed caption track to a video track.

from.title: Output configuration to use as parent.

id.title: ID(s) of the track(s) to use
id.description:
This ID can be a simple string or an object. The object allow to associate secondary track to the first one.
For now its only used to add a closed caption track to a video track.

major_brand.title: Major Brand
major_brand.description: A four character string that allows the use of a custom major brand.

embedded_parameter_sets.title: Embedded Parameter Sets.
embedded_parameter_sets.description: A flag to know whether parameter sets (VPS/SPS/PPS) are embedded in video (if true) or stored in a specific box in the MP4 file (if false).

progressive_download.title: Progressive Download
progressive_download.description: Set to enable progressive download mode.

from.title: Output configuration to use as parent.

id.title: ID(s) of the track(s) to use
id.description:
This ID can be a simple string or an object. The object allow to associate secondary track to the first one.
For now its only used to add a closed caption track to a video track.

smil_version.description:
specifies which definition of SMIL to comply to.
This essentially impacts how language & bitrate are represented in the manifest:
  - v1.0 uses hyphens between words (system-language / system-bitrate)
  - v2.0/v2.1/v3.0 use camelcase (systemLanguage / systemBitrate)

id.title: ID(s) of the track(s) to use
id.description: This ID can be a simple string or an object. The object allow to associate secondary track to the first one.

media_path.title: Media Path
media_path.description:
Sets the path of the stream fragments (see Output File Paths). File extension should be set according to the stream type:
  * video: .mp4
  * audio: .mp4
  * data (VTT subtitles): .vtt
Following wildcards can be used to define the media path template:
  * ${OUTPUT_BASE_URL}: Full path to the output folder of the url defined in __output__
  * ${OUTPUT_FILE_NAME}: Filename without extension of the url defined in __output__
  * ${TRACK_NAME}: Name of the track currently configured
  * ${EXT}: Extension automatically defined according to stream type

embedded_parameter_sets.title: Embedded Parameter Sets.
embedded_parameter_sets.description: A flag to know whether parameter sets (VPS/SPS/PPS) are embedded in video (if true) or stored in a specific box in the MP4 file (if false).

from.title: Output configuration to use as parent.

id.title: ID(s) of the track(s) to use
id.description:
This ID can be a simple string or an object. The object allow to associate secondary track to the first one.
For now its only used to add a closed caption track to a video track.

stream_type.title: Stream type
stream_type.description: Specifies the stream type used in the Program Map Table (PMT). Leave undefined or "standard" for standard-compliant automatic assignment, or set a numeric value to force a custom stream type. Setting a custom value is not recommended unless you need to handle very specific use cases.

minimum_bitrate.title: Minimum Bitrate
minimum_bitrate.description:
Sets a minimum bitrate that introduce padding (null packets) if sum of streams bitrates is inferior to this value.
Value `auto` will leave output bitrate follow sum of bitrate streams, otherwise value is specified as bits/s.

pcr_strategy.title: PCR Strategy
pcr_strategy.description:
Select the PCR behavior according to your needs:
- `auto`: Leave PCR untouched.
- `force_initial_pcr_to_zero`: Reset PCR to 0 at the beginning of the output transport stream.
  Not recommended for packaging to OTT formats as it may desynchronize PTS/DTS between profiles.
- `force_pcr_offset`: Force a higher PCR offset to avoid PCR rollover at the beginning of the output transport stream.
  Useful for packaging to OTT formats as it preserves PTS/DTS synchronization between profiles.

NB: When chunking is enabled, this parameter is ignored and the strategy is forced to `force_pcr_offset` to
avoid PCR rollover and preserve profiles synchronization.

enable.title: Enable Flag
enable.description: Enables the insertion of EBP boundary points in the TS stream.

explicit.title: Explicit EBP
explicit.description: Indicates whether each elementary stream shall explicitly carry the EBP structure in its adaptation private data field associated with boundary points.

transport_id.title: Transport Id
transport_id.description: Transport stream identifier, where 'auto' is replaced by a suitable default value.

program_number.title: Program Number
program_number.description: Program number, also called service id, where 'auto' is replaced by a suitable default value.

pat_period.title: Maximum PAT Period
pat_period.description: Maximum Program allocation table (PAT) transmission period, in seconds, where 'auto' is replaced by a suitable default value. The configured period is a maximum period, so in practice the actual period may be less.

pmt_period.title: Maximum PMT Period
pmt_period.description: Maximum Program map table (PMT) transmission period, in seconds, where 'auto' is replaced by a suitable default value. The configured period is a maximum period, so in practice the actual period may be less.

pmt_pid.title: PMT PID
pmt_pid.description: PMT PID, where 'auto' is replaced by a suitable default value.

pcr_pid.title: PCR PID
pcr_pid.description: PCR PID, where 'auto' implies that the PCR will be associated with one of the elementary stream (the video stream if there is one).

remove_padding.title: Remove Padding
remove_padding.description: Sets whether null packets shall be removed from the output transport stream. Padding is mandatory to produce constant bit rate streams.

remove_conformance_tables.title: Remove Conformance Tables
remove_conformance_tables.description: Sets whether auxiliary tables from DVB, ATSC or ISDB shall be removed from the output transport stream. This option must be disabled for full DVB, ATSC or ISDB conformance.

insert_pcr_on_pes.title: Insert PCR on PES
insert_pcr_on_pes.description: Sets whether PCR is inserted on every PES packet. This is mandatory for CableLabs compliance.

oneOf.title: Multiple Audio Frames per PES (deprecated)
oneOf.description: Sets whether each PES may contain several audio frames. In this case access unit start is not aligned on PES start, which is however mandatory for CableLabs compliance. Turning this flag off increases the output TS bitrate.

oneOf.title: Multiple Audio Frames per PES settings
oneOf.description: Settings concerning the fitting of audio access units in PES.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description:
Specifies a method of fitting access units in PES.
  - `disabled`:  The multiple audio frames mode is disabled. 1 access unit is put in each PES.
  - `pes_period`: The number of access units is controlled by the PES period.
  - `num_access_units`: A fixed number of access units is put in each PES.

num_access_units.title: Number of access units
num_access_units.description: This parameter is only useful for the _num_access_units_ mode. It specifies how many access units should be inserted in each PES.

insert_au_information.title: Insert DVB AU Packets
insert_au_information.description: Enables optional DVB AU packets. Some decoders may require this option to operate properly.

conformance.title: MPEG TS conformance
conformance.description: Selects the MPEG TS conformance to specifications beyond the MPEG standard.

strict_conformance.title: Strict Conformance
strict_conformance.description:
If set to true all the tracks used by this muxer will be verified and an error will be raised if one of the track
does not respect the requested conformance.

network_id.title: Network Id
network_id.description: Network identifier inserted in NIT tables, where 0 is replaced by a suitable default value.

network_name.title: Network Name
network_name.description: Network name inserted in NIT tables.

service_provider.title: Service Provider
service_provider.description: Service provider name inserted in SDT tables.

service_name.title: Service Name
service_name.description: Service name inserted in SDT tables.

nit_period.title: NIT Period
nit_period.description: Network information table (NIT) transmission period in seconds, where 0 is replaced by a suitable default value.

tdt_period.title: TDT Period
tdt_period.description: Time and date table (TDT) transmission period in seconds, where 0 is replaced by a suitable default value.

sdt_period.title: SDT Period
sdt_period.description: Service description table (SDT) transmission period in seconds, where 0 is replaced by a suitable default value.

mgt_period.title: MGT Period
mgt_period.description: Master guide table (MGT) transmission period in seconds, where 0 is replaced by a suitable default value.

stt_period.title: STT Period
stt_period.description: System time table (STT) transmission period in seconds, where 0 is replaced by a suitable default value.

vct_period.title: VCT Period
vct_period.description: Virtual channel table (VCT) transmission period in seconds, where 0 is replaced by a suitable default value.

discontinuity_indicator.title: Discontinuity Indicator
discontinuity_indicator.description:
Set discontinuity_indicator value of the first packet of a given stream: 
  - disabled: the discontinuity_indicator is not present.  
  - enabled_all_streams: the discontinuity_indicator is present for all streams, 
  - enabled_pcr_stream: the discontinuity_indicator is present only for the PCR stream (mandatory for CableLabs compliance).

disable_descriptors.title: Disable descriptors
disable_descriptors.description: List of all tag descriptors that will not be inserted in the muxer.

id.title: Workflow ID

progress.title: Workflow Progress
progress.description: The progress of the workflow in percentage.

status.title: Workflow Status
status.description:
This object contains information about states, times and durations.

Current and history states can be one of the following:
  * **pending**: the first step of the workflow is pending.
  * **running**: the first step of the workflow is or has been running.
  * **completed**: all the steps of the workflow are completed.
  * **aborted**: the job has been aborted.
  * **inerror**: a step of the workflow is in error.

CPU duration represents the sum of the CPU duration of all steps of the workflow.

steps.title: Steps List
steps.description:
This table contains the different steps necessary to perform the transcoding.
These steps are automatically created from the job configuration.

id.title: Step ID

name.title: Name
name.description: The user friendly name of the step.

progress.title: Step Progress
progress.description: The progress of the step in percentage.

status.title: Step Status
status.description:
This object contains information about states, times and durations.

Current and history states can be one of the following:
  * **pending**: the step is waiting for completion of the previous step
  and will be started as soon resources will be ready to execute the associated tasks.
  * **running**: at least one task of the step is or has been running.
  * **completed**: all the tasks of the step are completed.
  * **aborted**: the parent workflow has been aborted.
  * **inerror**: a task of the step is in error.

CPU duration represents the sum of the CPU duration of all tasks of the step.

tasks.title: Tasks List
tasks.description:
This array contains the different tasks necessary to perform the step.
These tasks are automatically created from the job configuration.

id.title: Task ID

name.title: Task name
name.description: The user friendly name of the task.

progress.title: Task Progress
progress.description: The progress of the task in percentage.

status.title: Task Status
status.description:
This object contains information about states, times and durations.

Current and history states can be one of the following:
  * **pending**: the task has just been created and waiting to be processed
  * **running**: the task is currently processed by a worker
  * **completed**: the worker that was processing the task has finished its job
  * **aborted**: the job has been aborted so the running tasks have been cancelled
  * **inerror**: an error has been detected
  * **expired**: the task has been expired

CPU duration represents an abstract metric of the CPU usage.
This is the product of the duration by the number of processor cores requested.
