system.title: System Information
system.description:
This object contains system information.

With the exception of the node property, the other fields are only
exposed for debugging purpose and can be ignored by users.

configuration.title: Task Configuration
configuration.description:
This object contains any data that the worker needs to process the task.

This field may be missing and is only exposed for debugging purpose.
It can be ignored by users.

stats.title: Stats List
stats.description:
This array contains a list of statistics reported by the processing worker.

This field may be missing and is only exposed for debugging purpose.
It can be ignored by users.

events.title: Events List
events.description:
This array contains a list of events reported by the processing worker.

This field may be missing and is only exposed for debugging purpose.
It can be ignored by users.

reason.title: Reason of the current state.
reason.description: Currently, the reason is only present with the current state "innerror".

reason.title: Reason of the state update.
reason.description: This field is absent if the reason is irrelevant.

start_date.title: Start Date
start_date.description:
UTC start date in seconds (when the state changes to running).

This field may be absent if the workflow has not been started.

end_date.title: End Date
end_date.description:
UTC end date in seconds (when the state changes to completed).

This field may be absent if the workflow has not been successfully processed.

duration.title: Duration
duration.description:
Processing duration in seconds.

This field may be absent if the workflow has not been successfully processed.

active_duration.title: Active Duration
active_duration.description:
Active processing duration in seconds (The amount of time that at least
one processing worker is running).

This field may be absent if the workflow has not been successfully processed.

cpu_duration.title: CPU Duration
cpu_duration.description:
CPU processing duration in seconds.

This field may be absent if the workflow has not been successfully processed.

version.description:
Version of the configuration.

If you do not provide the version, the data is considered to be formatted with version 1.0.
In this case, the configuration will be updated to the latest version.

id.title: Template unique identifier
id.description: This unique identifier is automatically generated at template creation.

name.title: Template name.
name.description:
Use this template name only for template creation. You can use this template name to make reference to the 
template when another template or job needs to use this template name. As you can use this template name as 
reference, this template name must be unique (no clones).

version.title: Version
version.description:
Version of the configuration.

If you do not provide the version, the data is considered to be formatted with version 1.0.
In this case, the configuration will be updated to the latest version.

tags.title: Tags list
tags.description: You can use tags for logical grouping of templates.

protected.title: Templete protection flag.
protected.description:
If set to true, the template cannot be modified or deleted.
This flag is used to protect defautl templates.

processing_resources_profile_id.title: Processing resources profile ID
processing_resources_profile_id.description:
The identifier that targets a resources profile ID in the Values of the helm chart (global.processing.resources.profiles.[id]). 
With this identifier, you can limit resources (memory and cpu) for processing tasks by selecting a user-defined profile in the charts Helm. 
For example, you may want to control workloads for some specifics Jobs.

cores.title: Cores
cores.description: The load of the job that determines the number of cores that must be reserved to make the job run

processing_queue.title: Processing Queue
processing_queue.description:
Podscaler queue used for all the processing pods of the job.
If the queue is stuck, jobs set on other queues will still be executed.
If the queue does not exist, the job will run on the podscaler `default` queue.

processing_node_affinity.title: Processing Node Affinity
processing_node_affinity.description:
List of labels to select a node where all the processing pods will run.
For the following explanations, we assume:
- required affinities are affinities with required_for_scheduling flag
- preferred affinities are affinities without required_for_scheduling flag
A node will be selected if all required affinities are equal to one of the configured values.
Moreover if some of those nodes are also satisfying all the preferred affinities, they will be chosen in priority.
WARNING: If no node is fulfilling all the required affinities, processing pods will never run and will block the whole jobs' queue.
If only preferred affinities are configured and no node is matching them, processing pods will be assigned to any other available node.
It is forbidden to configure more than 1 required affinity.
Example:
  We have the following configuration:
  - label = "usage", values = "transcoding, processing", required_for_scheduling=true
  - label = "capacity", values = "tiny, low", required_for_scheduling=false
  We have a 3-nodes cluster:
  - Node1 with labels "usage=application, capacity=low"
  - Node2 with labels "usage=transcoding, capacity=high"
  - Node3 with labels "usage=processing, capacity=tiny"
  All of those nodes have sufficient resources to host the processing pods.
  Node1 is discarded: its labels do not match the required affinity.
  Node2 can be selected because it matches the required affinity, but the preferred affinity is not fulfilled.
  Node3 fulfills both required and preferred affinities so it will be preferred over Node2.
  Therefore Node3 will be selected to run processing pods. 
  If Node3 is lacking resources, Node2 will be selected.
  If both Node3 and Node2 are lacking resources, processing pods will stay in a pending state until resources are freed, as Node1 can not be selected.

values.title: Values
values.description: Only one of the configured values is enough to fulfill the label affinity

required_for_scheduling.title: Required for Scheduling
required_for_scheduling.description:
If true, the affinity is a 'requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution' Kubernetes node affinity rule.
If false, the affinity is ANDed in the 'preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution' Kubernetes 
conditions with others preferred affinities.

using.title: Template reference
using.description:
This field specifies which template(s) you can use. This field can contain a single template name or a list of 
multiple templates names. 
You only need this field when you create a template or a job from templates. 
If this field is a list when you use each template from the list as a layer. 
These layers are applied according to the order of appearance in the list.

distributed_transcoding.title: Distributed transcoding configuration
distributed_transcoding.description: Distributed configuration, either as a number of parallel chunks, or a chunk duration.

chunk_number.title: Chunk number
chunk_number.description: The number of parallel chunks required to perform segment transcoding.

chunk_duration.title: Chunk duration
chunk_duration.description:
The maximum length of content in seconds that can be transcoded in a single chunk. The number of
parallel chunks to perform segment transcoding is then determined by the following formula:
`chunk_number = ceil(segment_duration / chunk_duration)`
Examples:
- for a segment of 300 seconds and a "chunk_duration" of 70 seconds we will obtain 5 chunks of 60 seconds.
- for a segment of 300 seconds and a "chunk_duration" of 120 seconds we will obtain 3 chunks of 100 seconds.
- for a segment of 300 seconds and a "chunk_duration" of 200 seconds we will obtain 2 chunks of 150 seconds.

ad_splicing.title: Ad Splicing
ad_splicing.description:
Enable ad splicing. With this option, audio frames will not be aligned on video frames.
Only works with TS muxers and with multiple_audio_frames_per_pes disabled.

smart_audio_video_alignment.title: Smart audio/video alignment
smart_audio_video_alignment.description:
When enabled, ensures that audio and video tracks have the exact same durations, duplicating video frames and inserting audio silence if required.
Only works in the use case complying to the following conditions:
- single video track with a framerate multiple of 25fps, with an encoding different than Dolby Vision
- only audio tracks that are encoded in AC-3 ("dolby_digital" and "dolby_digital_plus") or MPEG Layer 2 ("m2a"), using a samplerate of 48kHz or 32kHz
- TS muxer

custom_vars.title: Custom variables
custom_vars.description:
This object contains pairs of keys/values which can be used to substitute string values inside the job.
These strings can be both keys and values. To do this, the following wildcard format ${VAR_NAME} should be used inside the string value that you want to customize.

Example:
- we can define a file name in a custom var "FILE": "MyFile" then in the asset path "uri": "smb://my_server/sources/${FILE}.ts"
- we want to define dynamic track name "tracks": { "${MY_TRACK}": {...} }

Note that we have some limitations:
- redundant substitution are not supported. ex: {"MY_VAR": "${MY_VAR}"} or {"MY_VAR": "test", "MY_VAR2": "my_${MY_VAR}"}
- we can not substitute system words (keys or structures) ex: "assets" key, "progressif" enum value.
- usage of system wildcards are rejected.

url.title: Temporary directory URL
url.description:
Specifies the URL of the directory that will be used as temporary storage area.
If specified, temporary files will be held in this directory. Otherwise, temporary files will be held in job output paths.
This parameter has several applications:
- be able to hold temporary files on a storage that is not the one used for outputs.
- be able to support FTP outputs with features that require file seek operations (outputs of type mp4/mov/mxf and distributed transcoding)
Note that FTP storages (FTP, FTPS, and SFTP) are not supported due to their file seek limitations.

force_for_all_io_operations.title: Force use of the temporary directory for all I/O operations
force_for_all_io_operations.description:
When enabled, the temporary directory is also used as the I/O working directory.
This means that:
- input assets are copied to the temporary directory before processing
- transcoding output results are stored in the temporary directory before delivery

assets.title: Assets
assets.description:
This object contains the assets that can be referenced by tracks in inputs. Names of assets object are used to
reference them. The same asset can be referenced by multiple inputs tracks.

chunking.title: Chunking configuration
chunking.description:
This object contains the chunking configuration.
If you remove this object, you disable chunking.
2 configuration modes are possible:
- The period + jitter mode: The jitter is the allowed deviation to the nominal chunk period, to accomodate for scene cuts that occur near the chunk period.
Setting a jitter implies that the chunk boundary is placed up to half the jitter before or after the theorical boundary.
If _P_ is the nominal period and _J_ the jitter, the minimum chunk duration is _P_ - _J_ and the maximum is _P_ + _J_, as the boundary is inserted at multiples of _P_ plus or minus _J_/2.
If a chunk boundary is shifted from the nominal period, the following chunks boundaries will be computed in order to make up for the delta produced.
The consequence is the chunks number will be fixed.

- The min/max mode: Each chunk have a duration between the `minimum_chunk_duration` and `maximum_chunk_duration`.
There is no constraints to have a fixed number of chunks.
The chunk boundaries are computed following the scene cuts detected in a window beginning at _B_ + _MIN_ and going to _B_ + _MAX_ where _B_ is the end boundary of the previous chunk, _MIN_ the minimum chunk duration and _MAX_ the maximum chunk duration.

jitter.title: Jitter
jitter.description: Nominal chunk period jitter, in seconds.

additionalProperties.description: Video passthrough track is only mappable with an AVC input

ts.title: Mpeg2-TS outputs
ts.description: This object contains the configuration of each Mpeg2-TS output that must be generated by this

from.title: Output configuration to use as parent.

output.title: Output base URL
output.description: This URL is used as the reference for wildcards in media and initialization segment paths.

timescale.title: Timescale
timescale.description: Defines the timescale. If absent, it is automatically set from the video frame rate, from the audio sample rate or to a constant default value.

id.title: ID(s) of the track(s) to use
id.description:
This ID can be a simple string or an object. The object allow to associate secondary track to the first one.
For now it is only used to add a closed caption track to a video track.

media_path.title: Media Path
media_path.description:
Sets the path of the stream fragments (see Output File Paths).
Following wildcards can be used to define the media path template:
  * ${OUTPUT_BASE_URL}: Full path to the output folder of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${OUTPUT_FILE_NAME}: Filename without extension of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${TRACK_NAME}: Name of the track currently configured
  * ${CHUNK_NUMBER}: Segment index
  * ${TIMESCALE}: Segment timescale value.
  * ${TIME}: Segment time, in timescale units.
  * ${SECONDS}: Segment time, in seconds.
  * ${MILLISECONDS}: Segment time, in milliseconds.
  * ${MICROSECONDS}: Segment time, in microseconds.

initialization_segment_path.title: Initialization Segment Path
initialization_segment_path.description:
Sets the initialization segment path for the associated stream. 
Additional wildcards can be used to define initialization segment path template (cf media path).

_________common_definitions_output_track.title: Output Track Identifier
_________common_definitions_output_track.description: Output Track Identifier for this stream. If this field is zero or missing a default value will be used.

_________common_definitions_audio_channe.title: Audio Channel Policy
_________common_definitions_audio_channe.description:
Audio Channel Policy.
Defines whether the audio tracks will be split into mono-channel tracks or not

_________common_definitions_output_pid_y.title: Output PID
_________common_definitions_output_pid_y.description: Output PID for this stream. If not provided, a default suitable value is used.

_________common_definitions_server_drms_.description:
Server mode for DRM configuration.
Uses a Key Management Server to encrypt with one or more key(s)

key_management_server.title: Key management server
key_management_server.description: Name of the KMS server context, as it has been stored on the KMS server entrypoint.

system_ids.title: System IDs
system_ids.description:
List of system identifiers the encryption will be compatible with.
Unknown system identifiers are accepted, but mechanics specific to them may not be implemented.
Following system identifiers can be set and the corresponding ID will be used:
  * fairplay: 94ce86fb-07ff-4f43-adb8-93d2fa968ca2
  * vcas: 9a27dd82-fde2-4725-8cbc-4234aa06ec09
  * widevine: edef8ba9-79d6-4ace-a3c8-27dcd51d21ed
  * playready: 9a04f079-9840-4286-ab92-e65be0885f95

content_id.title: Content ID
content_id.description: Identifier that will be injected in the media.

encryption_method.title: Encryption method
encryption_method.description:
Specifies the encryption method. Supported methods depends of system IDs. The following methods are supported:
  * **cenc** : full sample encryption using AES standard with a 128-bit key and Counter mode (CTR)
  * **cbcs** : full audio sample encryption and video pattern encryption using AES standard with a 128-bit key
  and Cipher Block Chaining mode (CBC)
  * **auto** : let the KMS decide the encryption method

The following table shows the encryption methods supported by system IDs:

| System ID | fmp4 media segments |
| --------- | ------------------- |
| fairplay  | **cbcs**            |
| playready | **cbcs**, cenc      |
| widevine  | **cbcs**, cenc      |
| vcas      | **cbcs**, cenc      |
| others    | **cbcs**, cenc      |

keys.title: Keys
keys.description:
Keys Configuration.
Each key allows to encrypt a set of tracks

force_8_bytes_initialization_vector.title: Force 8-byte IVs
force_8_bytes_initialization_vector.description:
If set, it instructs the encrypter to use 8-byte IVs instead of the default 16-byte IVs.
This is a compatibility setting for some PlayReady players (versions 3.3 and below)
which do not support cenc content encrypted with 16-byte IVs.
It will be ignored for cbcs encryption method and the default 16-byte IVs will be used.
The use of this compatibility mode with another system ID is strongly discouraged because
the result cannot be guaranteed.

tracks.description: List of muxer tracks encrypted with this key.

_________common_definitions_manual_dash_.title: Manual Dash DRMs
_________common_definitions_manual_dash_.description:
Manual mode for DRM configuration.
Uses the configured key(s) to encrypt corresponding tracks.

system_ids.title: System IDs
system_ids.description:
List of system identifiers the encryption will be compatible with.
Unknown system identifiers are accepted, but mechanics specific to them may not be implemented.
Following system identifiers can be set and the corresponding ID will be used:
  * fairplay: 94ce86fb-07ff-4f43-adb8-93d2fa968ca2
  * vcas: 9a27dd82-fde2-4725-8cbc-4234aa06ec09
  * widevine: edef8ba9-79d6-4ace-a3c8-27dcd51d21ed
  * playready: 9a04f079-9840-4286-ab92-e65be0885f95

encryption_method.title: Encryption method
encryption_method.description:
Specifies the encryption method. Supported methods depends of system IDs. The following methods are supported:
  * **cenc** : full sample encryption using AES standard with a 128-bit key and Counter mode (CTR)
  * **cbcs** : full audio sample encryption and video pattern encryption using AES standard with a 128-bit key
  and Cipher Block Chaining mode (CBC)

The following table shows the encryption methods supported by system IDs:

| System ID | fmp4 media segments |
| --------- | ------------------- |
| fairplay  | **cbcs**            |
| playready | **cbcs**, cenc      |
| widevine  | **cbcs**, cenc      |
| vcas      | **cbcs**, cenc      |
| others    | **cbcs**, cenc      |

keys.title: Keys
keys.description:
Keys Configuration.
Each key allows to encrypt a set of tracks

kid.title: Key ID
kid.description:
KID is a key identifier that uniquely identifies the key needed to decrypt the associated samples
within the scope of an application so that KID is sufficient to identify a separately stored license
containing the key that was used to encrypt the content.

encryption_key.title: Encryption key
encryption_key.description: Key used to encrypt stream (16 bytes).

initialization_vector.title: Initialization vector
initialization_vector.description: Vector used during encryption (16 bytes). If not provided, an initialization vector will be generated.

system_ids_configuration.description: Key-value dictionary, with one key per System ID. Required for all system IDs
system_ids_configuration.title: System IDs configuration

additionalProperties.title: System ID configuration
additionalProperties.description: Configuration to be associated with the system ID

pssh.title: PSSH value
pssh.description: PSSH to be associated with the system ID.

tracks.description: List of muxer tracks encrypted with this key.

_________common_definitions_server_drms_.title: Key ID
_________common_definitions_server_drms_.description:
KID is a key identifier that uniquely identifies the key needed to decrypt the associated samples
within the scope of an application so that KID is sufficient to identify a separately stored license
containing the key that was used to encrypt the content. 

You can configure to **auto** to use an auto-generated KID.

_________common_definitions_encryption_m.title: Encryption method
_________common_definitions_encryption_m.description:
Specifies the encryption method. Supported methods depends of media segments format and system ID.

For media segments of type **ts**, the following methods are supported:
  * **aes_128**: complete encryption of the media segments using AES standard with a 128-bit key and Cipher Block Chaining mode (CBC)
  * **sample_aes**: full audio sample encryption and video pattern encryption using AES standard with a 128-bit key
  and Cipher Block Chaining mode (CBC)

For media segments of type **fmp4**, the following methods are supported:
  * **cenc** : full sample encryption using AES standard with a 128-bit key and Counter mode (CTR)
  * **cbcs** : full audio sample encryption and video pattern encryption using AES standard with a 128-bit key
  and Cipher Block Chaining mode (CBC)

The following table shows the encryption methods supported by system IDs as well as
default values **(in bold)** when the method is not specified:

| System ID | ts media segments       | fmp4 media segments |
| --------- | ----------------------- | ------------------- |
| fairplay  | **sample_aes**          | **cbcs**            |
| vcas      | **sample_aes**, aes_128 | **cbcs**, cenc      |
| widevine  | **sample_aes**, aes_128 | **cbcs**, cenc      |
| others    | **sample_aes**, aes_128 | **cbcs**, cenc      |

_________common_definitions_manual_drms_.title: PSSH data
_________common_definitions_manual_drms_.description: Protection System Specific Header box data, formatted as a base64-encoded string

_________common_definitions_manual_drms_.title: Initialization Vector
_________common_definitions_manual_drms_.description: Vector used during encryption (16 bytes). If not provided, an initialization vector will be generated.

_________common_definitions_manual_drms_.title: Key Format Version
_________common_definitions_manual_drms_.description:
The value is a quoted-string containing one or more positive integers separated by the "/" character (for example, "1", "1/2", or "1/2/5")
This relates to the optional key format attribute that can be specified in the EXT-X-KEY tag.

_________common_definitions_manual_drms_.title: Key URI
_________common_definitions_manual_drms_.description: URI containing the key’s value. Written in EXT-X-KEY:URI attribute in the playlist

_________common_definitions_manual_drms_.title: Key ID
_________common_definitions_manual_drms_.description: Encryption key identifier. To use only with fmp4 media format.

_________common_definitions_manual_drms_.title: Encryption Key
_________common_definitions_manual_drms_.description: Key used to encrypt stream (16 bytes).

_________common_definitions_manual_drms_.title: DRM System Identifier
_________common_definitions_manual_drms_.description: Protection system identifier. Unknown system identifiers are accepted, but mechanics specific to them may not be implemented.

_________common_definitions_hls_version_.title: HLS Version
_________common_definitions_hls_version_.description:
Sets the protocol version number. Currently supported versions are 3 through 12. If set to "auto", the version
will be automatically determined from the enabled features.

_________common_definitions_cmaf_muxer_y.title: CMAF Muxer
_________common_definitions_cmaf_muxer_y.description: CMAF Muxer can be used to produce fragmented mp4 media files in order to be referenced by HLS and/or DASH manifests.

from.title: Output configuration to use as parent.

timescale.title: Default Muxer Timescale
timescale.description: Defines the default muxer timescale, which will be applied to all tracks whose specific timescale is not specified. If absent, it is automatically set from the video frame rate, from the audio sample rate or to a constant default value.

enable_video_codec_switching.title: Enable Video Codec Switching
enable_video_codec_switching.description: Specifies whether different video codecs can be in the same adaptation set in the playlist or not.

enable_audio_codec_switching.title: Enable Audio Codec Switching
enable_audio_codec_switching.description: Specifies whether different audio codecs can be in the same adaptation set in the playlist or not.

id.title: ID(s) of the track(s) to use
id.description:
This ID can be a simple string or an object. The object allows to associate secondary track to the first one.
For now its only used to add a closed caption track to a video track.

media_path.title: Media Path
media_path.description:
Sets the path of the stream fragments (see Output File Paths). 
Fragment number will be automatically appended to the radix of the specified filename.
Following wildcards can be used to define the media path template:
  * ${OUTPUT_BASE_URL}: Full path to the output folder of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${OUTPUT_FILE_NAME}: Filename without extension of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${TRACK_NAME}: Name of the track currently configured
  * ${EXT}: "mp4"
Following wildcards are only useful in live profile:
  * ${CHUNK_NUMBER}: Segment index
  * ${TIME}: Segment time, in timescale units.

initialization_segment_path.title: Initialization Segment Path
initialization_segment_path.description:
Sets the initialization segment path for the associated stream. This section is only relevant with the
following configuration:
  * Live profile
  * Video or audio stream.
additional variables can be used to define initialization segment path template (cf media path)

media_playlist_path.title: Media Playlist Path
media_playlist_path.description:
Only used in HLS playlists.
Sets the path of the stream playlist, i.e. the playlist listing all the fragments of this stream
(see Output File Paths). File extension should be .m3u8.
Following wildcard is required to generate media playlists specific to each HLS playlist where the track will be used:
  * ${PLAYLIST_NAME}: Name of the HLS playlist it is used in
Following wildcards can be used to define the media path template:
  * ${OUTPUT_BASE_URL}: Full path to the output folder of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${OUTPUT_FILE_NAME}: Filename without extension of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${PROFILE_NAME}: Name of the profile in the HLS playlist it is used in
  * ${TRACK_NAME}: Name of the track currently configured
  * ${EXT}: "m3u8"

representation_id.title: Representation ID
representation_id.description:
Only used in DASH playlists.
Representation ID of output track in the playlist. If this field is missing, the track ID will be used to
present the track in the playlist.

subtitles_encapsulation.title: Subtitles Encapsulation
subtitles_encapsulation.description:
Only used with subtitles track.
Specifies subtitles encapsulation type: Raw or embedded in a MP4 container.

timescale.title: Specific Track Timescale
timescale.description: Defines a specific timescale for the track. If absent, the default muxer timescale (specified in the global muxer settings) will be used.

master_playlist_path.title: Master Playlist Path
master_playlist_path.description:
Sets the path of the master playlist. This master playlist will point to the media playlist matching with
the profiles listed in the presentation.
Following wildcards can be used to define the media path template:
  * ${OUTPUT_BASE_URL}: Full path to the output folder of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${OUTPUT_FILE_NAME}: Filename without extension of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${PLAYLIST_NAME}: Name of the HLS playlist it is used in
  * ${EXT}: "m3u8"

default_audio_track.title: Default Audio Track
default_audio_track.description: Indicates which audio track will be configured by the playlists manifests to be played by default.

default_subtitle_track.title: Default Subtitle Track
default_subtitle_track.description: Indicates which subtitle track will be configured by the playlists manifests to be played by default.

default_closed_caption_service.title: Default Closed Caption Service
default_closed_caption_service.description: Indicates which closed caption service will be configured by the playlists manifests to be played by default.

playlist_path.title: Iframes only playlist path
playlist_path.description:
Path where to write the playlist.
Following wildcards can be used to define the media path template:
  * ${OUTPUT_BASE_URL}: Full path to the output folder of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${OUTPUT_FILE_NAME}: Filename without extension of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${PLAYLIST_NAME}: Name of the current HLS playlist
  * ${PROFILE_NAME}: Name of the profile in this HLS playlist which the video track belongs to
  * ${TRACK_NAME}: Name of the video track from which the iframes playlist has been generated
  * ${EXT}: "m3u8"

profiles.title: Profiles description
profiles.description: Specify the profiles that will appear in the master playlist.

tracks.title: List of Muxer Tracks
tracks.description: The list of the Muxer tracks ID that must be used to create this profile.

manifest_path.title: Manifest Path
manifest_path.description:
Sets the path of the .mpd manifest.
Following wildcards can be used to define the media path template:
  * ${OUTPUT_BASE_URL}: Full path to the output folder of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${OUTPUT_FILE_NAME}: Filename without extension of the URL defined in __output__
  * ${PLAYLIST_NAME}: Name of the current DASH playlist
  * ${EXT}: "mpd"

preferential_language_code.title: Preferential Language Code
preferential_language_code.description:
Specifies which language code should be used preferentially in the manifest.
Passthrough will reuse the value specified in the track (from track settings, or from input language)

tracks.title: Tracks
tracks.description: List of tracks ids to reference in DASH manifest.

_________common_definitions_manual_cmaf_.title: Manual CMAF DRMs
_________common_definitions_manual_cmaf_.description:
Manual mode for DRM configuration.
Uses the configured key(s) to encrypt corresponding tracks.

system_ids.title: System IDs
system_ids.description:
List of system identifiers the encryption will be compatible with.
Unknown system identifiers are accepted, but mechanics specific to them may not be implemented.
Following system identifiers can be set and the corresponding ID will be used:
  * fairplay: 94ce86fb-07ff-4f43-adb8-93d2fa968ca2
  * vcas: 9a27dd82-fde2-4725-8cbc-4234aa06ec09
  * widevine: edef8ba9-79d6-4ace-a3c8-27dcd51d21ed
  * playready: 9a04f079-9840-4286-ab92-e65be0885f95

encryption_method.title: Encryption method
encryption_method.description:
Specifies the encryption method. Supported methods depends of system IDs. The following methods are supported:
  * **cenc** : full sample encryption using AES standard with a 128-bit key and Counter mode (CTR)
  * **cbcs** : full audio sample encryption and video pattern encryption using AES standard with a 128-bit key
  and Cipher Block Chaining mode (CBC)

The following table shows the encryption methods supported by system IDs:

| System ID | fmp4 media segments |
| --------- | ------------------- |
| fairplay  | **cbcs**            |
| playready | **cbcs**, cenc      |
| widevine  | **cbcs**, cenc      |
| vcas      | **cbcs**, cenc      |
| others    | **cbcs**, cenc      |

keys.title: Keys
keys.description:
Keys Configuration.
Each key allows to encrypt a set of tracks

kid.title: Key ID
kid.description:
KID is a key identifier that uniquely identifies the key needed to decrypt the associated samples
within the scope of an application so that KID is sufficient to identify a separately stored license
containing the key that was used to encrypt the content.

encryption_key.title: Encryption key
encryption_key.description: Key used to encrypt stream (16 bytes).

initialization_vector.title: Initialization vector
initialization_vector.description: Vector used during encryption (16 bytes). If not provided, an initialization vector will be generated.

system_ids_configuration.description: Key-value dictionary, with one key per System ID
system_ids_configuration.title: System IDs configuration

additionalProperties.title: System ID configuration
additionalProperties.description: Configuration to be associated with the system ID

pssh.title: PSSH value
pssh.description: PSSH to be associated with the system ID. Required for all system IDs except fairplay

key_uri.title: Key URI
key_uri.description:
Specific to HLS playlists.
URI containing the key’s value.
Written in EXT-X-KEY:URI attribute in the media playlist

key_format.title: Key format
key_format.description:
Specific to HLS playlists.
Quoted-string that specifies how the key is represented in the resource identified by the URI.
Its absence indicates an implicit value of "identity". 
Written in EXT-X-KEY:KEYFORMAT attribute in the media playlist.

key_format_version.title: Key format version
key_format_version.description:
Specific to HLS playlists.
Quoted-string containing one or more positive integers separated by the "/" character (for example, "1", "1/2", or "1/2/5")
This relates to the optional key format attribute that can be specified in the EXT-X-KEY tag.
Written in EXT-X-KEY:KEYFORMATVERSIONS attribute in the media playlist.

tracks.description: List of muxer tracks encrypted with this key.

_________common_definitions_input_overri.title: Override Policy
_________common_definitions_input_overri.description:
Sets when the override must occur:
- `never` nothing done to override any existing parameter or fill a missing one
- `missing` sibling parameter will be set only if not exist in the source
- `always` value will be set regardless of the source

preset.title: Preset
preset.description: Preset value. If not given, the first preset linked to the license will be used.

payload.title: Payload
payload.description: A 1/10/22/24 bit integer that is the payload being embedded in the video. The length of the payload depends on the sdk and preset.

_________common_definitions_track_video_.title: First timecode override
_________common_definitions_track_video_.description:
First timecode values are taken from input with the following priority:
  - muxer: timecode signalized by the muxer layer (i.e. Material timecode from MXF)
  - video track: timecode signaled in the video SEI
Those values can be overriden with this parameter.
Configured value will be signalized in the video track and in the muxer (timecode track for MOV, Material package for MXF)

_________common_definitions_timecode_val.title: Timecode Value
_________common_definitions_timecode_val.description:
Timecode value according to SMPTE 12M standard in "HH:MM:SS:FF" format where the last ':' can be replaced by ','
or ';' to indicate frame drop for 29.97fps and 59.94fps sources.
The frame rate in the timecode will be automatically measured from the input file.

Note that the representing frame will be included in the segment when used as a start indication, but excluded
when used as an end indication.

_________common_definitions_track_video_.title: SEI 3D Override
_________common_definitions_track_video_.description:
This field only works with AVC and HEVC codecs.
The format with __policy__ and __value__ fields is now deprecated.
Please, use the new format by setting this field as a simple string from the following values:
* auto: SEI 3D metadata are taken from input (similar to a __policy__ set to 'never' in the former format)
* side_by_side: the left part of a frame is one view and the right one another view
* top_bottom: the top part of a frame is one view and the bottom one another view

NB: with former format, *missing* __policy__ has now the same effect than *never* (similar to *auto* in the new format)

value.title: SEI 3D Value
value.description:
SEI 3D configurable value.
side_by_side value means that the left part of a frame is one view and the right one another view.
top_bottom value means that the top part of a frame is one view and the bottom one another view.

_________common_definitions_track_video_.title: Colorimetry Parameters Overrides
_________common_definitions_track_video_.description: Some colorimetry signaling values in output are taken from inputs. These can be overriden thanks to following parameters.

mastering_display_color_volume.title: Mastering Display Color Volume
mastering_display_color_volume.description:
Overrides mastering display color volume SEI as specified in ST 2086.
The format with __policy__ and __value__ fields is now deprecated.
Please, use the new format by choosing between:
* auto: Mastering Display Color Volume signaling is taken from the input
* custom: Set __color_primaries__, __maximum_display_mastering_luminance__ and __minimum_display_mastering_luminance__ fields

NB: with former format, *missing* __policy__ has now the same effect than *never* (similar to *auto* in the new format)

content_light_level_information.title: Content Light Level Information
content_light_level_information.description:
Overrides content light level information in SEI.
The format with __policy__ and __value__ fields is now deprecated.
Please, use the new format by choosing between:
* auto: Content Light Level Information signaling is taken from the input
* custom: Set __maximum_content_light_level__ and __maximum_frame_average_light_level__ fields

NB: with former format, *missing* __policy__ has now the same effect than *never* (similar to *auto* in the new format)

maximum_content_light_level.title: Maximum Content Light Level
maximum_content_light_level.description: Maximum content light level in nits (cd/m^2).

maximum_frame_average_light_level.title: Maximum Frame Average Light Level
maximum_frame_average_light_level.description: Maximum frame average light level in nits (cd/m^2).

maximum_content_light_level.title: Maximum Content Light Level
maximum_content_light_level.description: Maximum content light level in nits (cd/m^2).

maximum_frame_average_light_level.title: Maximum Frame Average Light Level
maximum_frame_average_light_level.description: Maximum frame average light level in nits (cd/m^2).

oneOf.title: Custom Color Primaries
oneOf.description: Components and white point chromacity coordinates in XYZ space.

components.title: Display Primaries
components.description:
Array of the color primaries for the mastering display.
These values can be set thanks to four decimal in range [0.0000, 1.0000].

white_point.title: White Point
white_point.description: Nominal chromacity of the white point of the mastering display.

maximum_display_mastering_luminance.title: Maximum Display Mastering Luminance
maximum_display_mastering_luminance.description:
Nominal maximum display luminance of the mastering display, represented in nits (cd/m^2).
Value shall be a number with up to five significant digits and up to two decimal places in range [0000.00, 10000.00]

minimum_display_mastering_luminance.title: Minimum Display Mastering Luminance
minimum_display_mastering_luminance.description:
Nominal minimum display luminance of the mastering display, represented in nits (cd/m^2).
Value shall be a number with up to three significant digits and up to four decimal places in range [000.0000, 999.9999]

_________common_definitions_video_color_.title: Color Primary
_________common_definitions_video_color_.description: Pair of X,Y coordinates for the current color. These values can be set thanks to four decimal in range [0.0000, 1.0000].

oneOf.title: Custom Color Primaries
oneOf.description: Components and white point chromacity coordinates in XYZ space.

components.title: Display Primaries
components.description:
Array of the color primaries for the mastering display.
These values can be set thanks to four decimal in range [0.0000, 1.0000].

white_point.title: White Point
white_point.description: Nominal chromacity of the white point of the mastering display.

maximum_display_mastering_luminance.title: Maximum Display Mastering Luminance
maximum_display_mastering_luminance.description:
Nominal maximum display luminance of the mastering display, represented in nits (cd/m^2).
Value shall be a number with up to five significant digits and up to two decimal places in range [0000.00, 10000.00]

minimum_display_mastering_luminance.title: Minimum Display Mastering Luminance
minimum_display_mastering_luminance.description:
Nominal minimum display luminance of the mastering display, represented in nits (cd/m^2).
Value shall be a number with up to three significant digits and up to four decimal places in range [000.0000, 999.9999]

text_size.title: Font Size
text_size.description: Font size, in pixels. Defaults to 5% of screen height. This value is used only for subtitles for which the layout is unknown.

background_color.title: Background Color
background_color.description: Background color. If not present the color will not be overridden.

text_color.title: Font Color
text_color.description: Font color of the text. If not present the color will not be overridden.

_________common_definitions_safe_area_co.title: Safe Area
_________common_definitions_safe_area_co.description: Rectangular area within the screen where subtitles may be properly displayed.

top_margin.title: Top Margin
top_margin.description: Margin, in percentage of the screen width, from the top edge of the picture.

bottom_margin.title: Bottom Margin
bottom_margin.description: Margin, in percentage of the screen width, from the bottom edge of the picture.

left_margin.title: Left Margin
left_margin.description: Margin, in percentage of the screen width, from the left edge of the picture.

right_margin.title: Right Margin
right_margin.description: Margin, in percentage of the screen width, from the right edge of the picture.

device_id.title: Device ID
device_id.description: Device ID, as inserted in Nielsen info (fixed value matching product name)

vendor_id.title: Vendor ID
vendor_id.description: Vendor ID, as inserted is Nielsen bitstream (fixed value matching ATEME)

dash.title: DASH Subtitle Signaling
dash.description:
Role defines and describes the characteristics and/or structural functions of a subtitle component.

Accessibility defines a general term used to describe the degree to which a subtitle component
is available to as many people as possible. It fulfil a very similar purpose with respect to subtitle content
components as for role but is specifically intended for accessibility.

Notes:
- DASH subtitle signaling is intended for subtitle components in DASH outputs

oneOf.title: Auto
oneOf.description: Auto mode, application will use a value according to track property

oneOf.title: Discard
oneOf.description: Value to discard subtitle accessibility signaling

oneOf.title: Auto
oneOf.description: Auto mode, application will use a value of type "DVB DASH audio purpose" according to track property

_________common_definitions_dash_audio_p.title: DASH Audio Purpose
_________common_definitions_dash_audio_p.description: DVB DASH audio purpose values as defined by ETSI TS 102 822-3-1 (SchemeUri="urn:tva:metadata:cs:AudioPurposeCS:2007")

_________common_definitions_dash_subtitl.title: DASH Subtitle Role
_________common_definitions_dash_subtitl.description: Subtitle role values as defined by ISO/IEC 23009-1 (SchemeUri="urn:mpeg:dash:role:2011")

font_scale.title: Font Scale
font_scale.description: Font scale, relative to subtitle line height.

ignore_double_height.title: Ignore Double Height
ignore_double_height.description:
Ignore double height subtitles.
Subtitle sources such as EBU STL may contain a double height control code to indicate that the subtitle row will occupy two lines when output.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description:
The ttml configuration mode is used for selecting the output type of the subtitles. 2 configuration modes are possible:
- The image mode: allows to generate smpte image subtitles from teletext, webvtt subtitles or dvb subtitles.
- The text mode: allows to generate ttml text subtitles from teletext, webvtt subtitles or dvb subtitles.
- The auto mode: If this mode is set, smpte image subtitles will be generated from dvb subtitles and ttml text subtitles will be generated from teletext or webvtt subtitles.
Note that, when generating image ttml from dvb subtitles, we re-use the images from the dvb subtitles without applying any additional formatting.

_________common_definitions_safe_area_co.title: Safe Area
_________common_definitions_safe_area_co.description: If present, defines the rectangular area within the screen where subtitles may be displayed.

top_margin.title: Top Margin
top_margin.description: Margin, in percentage of the screen width, from the top edge of the picture.

bottom_margin.title: Bottom Margin
bottom_margin.description: Margin, in percentage of the screen width, from the bottom edge of the picture.

left_margin.title: Left Margin
left_margin.description: Margin, in percentage of the screen width, from the left edge of the picture.

right_margin.title: Right Margin
right_margin.description: Margin, in percentage of the screen width, from the right edge of the picture.

_________common_definitions_subtitle_max.title: Maximum Displayable Columns
_________common_definitions_subtitle_max.description:
Configure the maximum number of displayed columns. At least 1 column must be displayable.
Be careful, all tracks mapped to same input must have the same configured value for this parameter.
This parameter has only an effect if the input is mapped to an STL asset with DSC 0.

_________common_definitions_subtitle_max.title: Maximum Displayable Rows
_________common_definitions_subtitle_max.description:
Configure the maximum number of displayed rows. At least 1 row must be displayable.
Be careful, all tracks mapped to same input must have the same configured value for this parameter.
This parameter has only an effect if the input is mapped to an STL asset with DSC 0.

from.title: Track configuration to use as parent.

atsc.title: Enable ATSC Captions
atsc.description:
Enables embedding of CEA-608/CEA-708 captions in ATSC wrapping mode.
The way to embed these captions can be configured with the **atsc_standard** parameter.

atsc_standard.title: ATSC Captions Encapsulation Standard
atsc_standard.description:
The embedded source captions may be CEA-608 captions or CEA-708 captions or both CEA-608 and CEA-708.
When ATSC captions are enabled, it is possible to specify how these captions should be handled when the encoder ingests content.
The following table describes the behavior for various scenarios.

| CEA-608 in Source | CEA-708 in Source | Carriage Standard   | Result                                                                                                                                                                     |
| ----------------- | ------------------| --------------------| -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Yes               | No                | cea608              | Original CEA-608 is preserved as 608-compatibility bits in the CEA-708 data.                                                                                               |
| Yes               | No                | cea708              | Original CEA-608 is preserved as 608-compatibility bits in the CEA-708 data.                                                                                               |
| Yes               | No                | upconvert to cea708 | CEA-708 data is created based on the CEA-608 data and CEA-608 data is added as 608-compatibility bits in the CEA-708 data.                                                 |
| No                | Yes               | cea608              | CEA-708 data is discarded. No output captions.                                                                                                                             |
| No                | Yes               | cea708              | Original CEA-708 is preserved.                                                                                                                                             |
| No                | Yes               | upconvert to cea708 | Not recommended. CEA-708 data is discarded. New CEA-708 data is created with null captions.                                                                                |
| Yes               | Yes               | cea608              | CEA-708 data is discarded. Original CEA-608 is preserved as 608-compatibility bits in the CEA-708 data.                                                                    |
| Yes               | Yes               | cea708              | Original CEA-608 is preserved and original CEA-708 is preserved.                                                                                                           |
| Yes               | Yes               | upconvert to cea708 | Not recommended. CEA-708 data is discarded. New CEA-708 data is created based on the CEA-608 data and CEA-608 data is added as 608-compatibility bits in the CEA-708 data. |

scte20.title: Enable SCTE-20 Captions
scte20.description: Enables embedding of CEA-608 captions in SCTE-20 wrapping mode into an MPEG-2 video stream.

dash.title: DASH Closed Captions Signaling
dash.description:
Specifies values for the "Accessibility" tag of any video containing this closed captions track.
The "Accessibility" tag will be inserted if and only if configured here.
Signaling will be done following guidelines from ANSI/SCTE 214-1 2022.

cea608.description: List of CEA-608 Closed caption channels to announce.

channel.description: Identifier of the channel. Expected to be unique.

language.description:
ISO 639 language code for this channel. 
The language tag will only be inserted if this parameter is set.

cea708.description: List of CEA-708 Closed caption channels to announce.

service.description: Identifier of the service. Expected to be unique.

language.description:
ISO 639 language code for this service.
The language tag will only be inserted if this parameter is set.

page_description.description:
Configurations for each page to insert in the teletext.
List length must match the number of inputs in the associated track.
Each config will be applied to an input, in the same order as they're defined here and in the track inputs.

page_code.description: Teletext magazine/page code. Must be unique among all page (including initial) codes.

from.title: Track configuration to use as parent.

text_size.title: Font Size
text_size.description: Font size, in pixels. Defaults to 5% of screen height. This value is used only for subtitles for which the layout is unknown.

screen.title: Screen Size
screen.description: This parameter overrides the target size of the screen. If missing, the highest resolution from the video tracks used along this DVB Subtitles will be chosen. Highest video resolution is chosen as the highest height, using the highest width as a tiebreaker.

line_by_line.title: Line By Line
line_by_line.description: Generates one bitmap per line, and adjusts its size to its line content. This option optimizes bitrate usage.

type.title: Subtitle Type
type.description: Type of the subtitle, defines the display aspect ratio (DAR) and whether the subtitle is for hard of hearing people or not.

acquisition_delay.title: Acquisition Delay
acquisition_delay.description: Delay between two PES acquisition time point.

max_bitrate.title: Maximum Bitrate
max_bitrate.description: Maximum output bitrate, in bits/s. If the input stream bitrate is above this value, extra bits will be thrown away, hence the value should be as high as the maximum input bitrate expected.

vbv_size.title: Vbv size
vbv_size.description: Vbv size in bits. Default value is defined by maximum bitrate.

page_timeout.title: Page timeout
page_timeout.description: Maximum display duration (in seconds) for a subtitle if no update is received

from.title: Track configuration to use as parent.

conformance.title: Conformance
conformance.description: xdcamhd conformance enforces a sample_rate of 48kHz

joint_stereo.title: Joint Stereo
joint_stereo.description: When set, joint stereo coding mode may be used if suitable.

copyright_flag.title: Copyright Flag
copyright_flag.description: When set, the informative copyright flag is set in the output audio bitstream.

original_flag.title: Original Flag
original_flag.description: When set, the informative original flag is set in the output audio bitstream.

_________common_definitions_loudness_yam.title: Loudness Control Parameters
_________common_definitions_loudness_yam.description:
Loudness processing parameters
Missing loudness control parameters node will results in disabling loudness processing.
Loudness normalization only supports an output sample rate of 48000 Hz.

_________common_definitions_loudness_gai.title: Gain Configuration
_________common_definitions_loudness_gai.description: Gain is the process scaling the audio samples to roughly and quickly change the audio volume.

oneOf.title: Normalization Mode
oneOf.description:
Loudness measurement is compliant with ITU-R BS.1770.
Predefined sets of loudness normalization parameters Target Level and Maximum True Peak Level 
will be used as follow:

| Enum      | Description | Target Level | Maximum True Peak Level |
| --------- | ------------| ------------ | ----------------------- |
| ebu_r128  | EBU R 128   | -23 LUFS     | -1 dBTP                 |
| atsc_a85  | ATSC A/85   | -24 LKFS     | -2 dBTP                 |

oneOf.title: Custom Normalization
oneOf.description:
Target Level and Maximum True Peak Level can be set independently.
Loudness measurement is compliant with ITU-R BS.1770.

target.title: Target Loudness
target.description: Loudness normalization target level, in LUFS/LKFS.

_________common_definitions_loudness_tru.title: Maximum True Peak Level
_________common_definitions_loudness_tru.description: Maximum allowed true peak level, in dBTP.

_________common_definitions_audio_stereo.title: Stereo downmix mode
_________common_definitions_audio_stereo.description:
Dolby algorithm to use when downmixing a 5.1/7.1 to stereo/mono audio track.
* auto: automatically selects downmix mode using input stream metadata. When no metadata is present, Lo/Ro is used.
* Lo/Ro: downmix to conventional stereo signal. Keeps surround information when downmixing to mono.
* Lt/Rt: downmix to surround stereo signal. Looses surround information when downmixing to mono.

kantar_server_context.title: Kantar server context
kantar_server_context.description: Name of the Kantar server context, as it has been stored on the Kantar server entrypoint.

unique_content_id.title: Unique content ID
unique_content_id.description: Content reference used for the watermarking.

channel_name.title: Channel name
channel_name.description: Channel name used for the watermarking. This channel must be present in the specified Kantar server, and can be 20 characters at most.

license_id.title: License ID
license_id.description:
License ID used for the watermarking. 
The license must be present in the specified Kantar server, and also contain the specified channel name. 
If left empty, a license with the specified channel will be arbitrarily selected from the server.

report_path.title: Report path
report_path.description:
Directory where the watermarking report will be written. 
If left empty, the directory of an arbitrary output using this track will be selected.

stream_name.title: Stream name
stream_name.description:
Stream name which will be used in the watermarking report.
If left empty, the track ID of an arbitrary track using this Kantar configuration will be used.

offset.title: Offset
offset.description: Integral number of seconds from where to start the watermarking.

from.title: Track configuration to use as parent.

sample_format.title: Sample Format
sample_format.description: Sample format of the PCM track. This parameter is only applied when the PCM track is muxed in a MOV output.

joint_stereo.title: Joint Stereo
joint_stereo.description: When set, joint stereo coding mode may be used if suitable.

copyright_flag.title: Copyright Flag
copyright_flag.description: When set, the informative copyright flag is set in the output audio bitstream.

original_flag.title: Original Flag
original_flag.description: When set, the informative original flag is set in the output audio bitstream.

from.title: Track configuration to use as parent.

joint_stereo.title: Joint Stereo
joint_stereo.description: When set, joint stereo coding mode may be used if suitable.

copyright_flag.title: Copyright Flag
copyright_flag.description: When set, the informative copyright flag is set in the output audio bitstream.

original_flag.title: Original Flag
original_flag.description: When set, the informative original flag is set in the output audio bitstream.

_________common_definitions_audio_signal.title: TS Audio Signaling
_________common_definitions_audio_signal.description:
Audio signaling in TS output.    
Signaling can be configured in the DVB supplementary_audio descriptor, i.e. for audio_description purposes.

Important: TS audio signaling is intended for audio components in TS outputs only.

supplementary_audio.title: Supplementary audio descriptor
supplementary_audio.description:
Supplementary Audio Descriptor signaling. Only compliant in TS outputs with DVB conformance. supplementary_audio descriptor will not be created in the output with the following conditions: - this parameter is not set - the audio track is normal (no broadcast-mix configured) In the example above, the descriptor will be passthrough if present in the input, absent otherwise. Configuring _audio_purpose_ will force the **mix_type** and **editorial_classification** as described in the table below.
Configuring _language_ will force the **language_code_present** to 1 and **ISO_639_language_code** to the configured value. Below are the corresponding values for _audio_purpose_ regarding the configured signaling: | Signaling | mix_type | editorial_classification | |:-----------------------:|:--------:|:------------------------:| | main | 1 | 0x00 | | audio_description_bmix | 1 | 0x01 | | audio_description_rmix | 0 | 0x01 | | clean_audio_bmix | 1 | 0x02 | | parametric_data | 0 | 0x04 | | spoken_subtitles_bmix | 1 | 0x03 | | spoken_subtitles_rmix | 0 | 0x03 | Here is the behavior for _audio_purpose_ and _language_ parameters if they are not set. If _audio_purpose_ is not set: * normal audio track: the audio track is considered as 'main' so **mix_type** & **editorial_classification** are set accordingly * broadcast-mix audio track: the audio track is considered as 'audio_description_bmix' so **mix_type** & **editorial_classification** are set accordingly If _language_ is not set (for the paragraph below, a language is considered as overridden if it is configured in the _overrides_ section of the track): * no language is probed and language is not overridden or overridden to "NAR"/"qad": - **language_code_present** set to 0 * a language is probed and language is overridden to "NAR"/"qad" or is not overridden: - **language_code_present** set to 1 - **ISO_639_language_code** set to the probed language from the input * language is overridden: - **language_code_present** set to 1 - **ISO_639_language_code** set to the overridden value You must configure _language_ if you want to set a different value than the probed and the overridden ones. Example: You want to signal the audio track with "eng" language in the following case: - probed language is "fre" - the overridden language is "qad" to signal an audio_description track to legacy receivers - you must configure _language_ to "eng" Note: - In order to signal the track as "audio_description" for legacy receivers, configure the language to "NAR" or "qad" in _overrides_ section of the track

audio_purpose.title: Audio purpose
audio_purpose.description: Audio Purpose forcing **mix_type** and **editorial_classification** in the descriptor.

_________common_definitions_audio_signal.title: DASH Audio Signaling
_________common_definitions_audio_signal.description:
Role defines and describes the characteristics and/or structural functions of an audio component.

Accessibility defines a general term used to describe the degree to which an audio component
is available to as many people as possible. It fulfil a very similar purpose with respect to audio content
components as for role but is specifically intended for accessibility.

Notes:
- DASH audio signaling is intended for audio components in DASH outputs
- if any accessibility, the `auto` mode will use values of type "DVB DASH audio purpose"

oneOf.title: Auto
oneOf.description: Auto mode, application will use a value according to track property

oneOf.title: Discard
oneOf.description: Value to discard audio accessibility signaling

oneOf.title: Auto
oneOf.description: Auto mode, application will use a value of type "DVB DASH audio purpose" according to track property

_________common_definitions_dash_audio_r.title: DASH Audio Role
_________common_definitions_dash_audio_r.description: Audio role values as defined by ISO/IEC 23009-1 (SchemeUri="urn:mpeg:dash:role:2011")

_________common_definitions_dolby_atmos_.title: OBI Atmos configuration
_________common_definitions_dolby_atmos_.description: Atmos configuration for Object Base Immersive mode

from.title: Track configuration to use as parent.

metering_mode.title: Metering Mode
metering_mode.description: Loudness metering mode. Loudness measurement is compliant with ITU-R BS.1770.

speech_threshold.title: Speech Threshold
speech_threshold.description: Speech threshold. Percentage of speech needed to switch between speech loudness or overall program loudness.

dynamic_range_control.title: Dynamic Range Control Settings
dynamic_range_control.description: Specifies whether the dynamic range control settings should be determined by metadata in the source or by the user. If present, dynamic range control settings will be overriden by user's value. If not present, dynamic range control settings will be automatically applied (metadata in the source or default values).

line_mode_profile.title: Line mode profile
line_mode_profile.description: Indicates the dynamic range control profile (compression curve and related parameters) to use when playing back content in Line mode.

rf_mode_profile.title: RF mode profile
rf_mode_profile.description: Indicates the dynamic range control profile (compression curve and related parameters) to use when playing back content in RF mode.

downmix.title: Downmix Settings
downmix.description: Specifies whether the downmix settings should be determined by metadata in the source or by the user. If present, downmix settings will be overriden by user's value. If not present, downmix settings will be automatically applied (metadata in the source or default values).

loro_center_downmix_gain.title: Lo/Ro center downmix gain
loro_center_downmix_gain.description: Indicates the gain (in dB) that a decoder applies to the Center channel before adding it to the Left and Right channels while performing an Lo/Ro downmix.

loro_surround_downmix_gain.title: Lo/Ro surround downmix gain
loro_surround_downmix_gain.description: Indicates the gain (in dB) that a decoder applies to the surround channels before adding them to the Left and Right channels while performing an Lo/Ro downmix.

ltrt_center_downmix_gain.title: Lt/Rt center downmix gain
ltrt_center_downmix_gain.description: Indicates the gain (in dB) that a decoder applies to the Center channel before adding it to the Left and Right channels while performing an Lt/Rt downmix.

ltrt_surround_downmix_gain.title: Lt/Rt surround downmix gain
ltrt_surround_downmix_gain.description: Indicates the gain (in dB) that a decoder applies to the surround channels before adding them to the Left and Right channels while performing an Lt/Rt downmix.

preferred_stereo_downmix_mode.title: Preferred stereo downmix mode
preferred_stereo_downmix_mode.description: Indicates the method a decoder should use when downmixing the audio for playback.

from.title: Track configuration to use as parent.

line_mode_profile.title: Line mode profile
line_mode_profile.description: Indicates the dynamic range control profile (compression curve and related parameters) to use when playing back content in Line mode.

rf_mode_profile.title: RF mode profile
rf_mode_profile.description: Indicates the dynamic range control profile (compression curve and related parameters) to use when playing back content in RF mode.

loro_center_downmix_gain.title: Lo/Ro center downmix gain
loro_center_downmix_gain.description: Indicates the gain (in dB) that a decoder applies to the Center channel before adding it to the Left and Right channels while performing an Lo/Ro downmix.

loro_surround_downmix_gain.title: Lo/Ro surround downmix gain
loro_surround_downmix_gain.description: Indicates the gain (in dB) that a decoder applies to the surround channels before adding them to the Left and Right channels while performing an Lo/Ro downmix.

ltrt_center_downmix_gain.title: Lt/Rt center downmix gain
ltrt_center_downmix_gain.description: Indicates the gain (in dB) that a decoder applies to the Center channel before adding it to the Left and Right channels while performing an Lt/Rt downmix.

ltrt_surround_downmix_gain.title: Lt/Rt surround downmix gain
ltrt_surround_downmix_gain.description: Indicates the gain (in dB) that a decoder applies to the surround channels before adding them to the Left and Right channels while performing an Lt/Rt downmix.

surround_downmix_gain.title: Surround downmix gain
surround_downmix_gain.description: Indicates the gain (in dB) applied to surround content only when content is rendered to 5.1.

height_downmix_gain.title: Height downmix gain
height_downmix_gain.description: Indicates the gain (in dB) applied to height content only when content is rendered to 5.1.

preferred_stereo_downmix_mode.title: Preferred stereo downmix mode
preferred_stereo_downmix_mode.description: Indicates the method a decoder should use when downmixing the audio for playback.

surround_phase_shift.title: Surround phase shift
surround_phase_shift.description: Indicates whether a 90-degree phase-shift filter is applied to the surround channels to prevent cancellation in a subsequent surround-compatible downmix applied to the decoded signal.

_________common_definitions_loudness_dol.title: Atmos Loudness parameters
_________common_definitions_loudness_dol.description: Atmos loudness processing parameters. Note that normalization and metadata update from source metering are only available for 5.1.4 channel mode. Note that normalization and metadata update from source metering will be ignored for pass-through channel mode when input is 7.1.4.

correction_mode.description: Specifies whether the Dolby professional loudness correction library should modify the audio content or (alternatively) author metadata for a downstream decoder to use as the basis for leveling or correcting the audio.

dialnorm_edition_policy.description:
If policy is "always", metadata update will always happen.
If policy is "missing", the metadata update will only happen if the input doesn't have a reliable dialogue normalization.
The exact conditions for this last case are:
  - this track has a non Dolby Digital or non Dolby E input in any input segment
  - this track uses aggregation in any input segment
  - this track has no layout change between input and output in each input segment
  - this track has any input with a "audio_channel" filter

dialnorm_source.description:
Describes how the new dialogue normalization is determined when metadata update happens:
- If Dialnorm Source is set to Source Metering, the dialnorm will reflect actual loudness measured from the audio signal
- If Dialnorm Source is set to User Input, the dialnom will be the one configured in the associated config structure

user_dialogue_normalization.description: When Dialnorm Source is set to user input, the target value can be configured through this field

correction_mode.description: Specifies whether the Dolby professional loudness correction library should modify the audio content or (alternatively) author metadata for a downstream decoder to use as the basis for leveling or correcting the audio.

regulation_type.description:
The Regulation Type specifies the loudness practice with which the leveled audio must comply.
Sometimes called loudness practice or target loudness regulation. 
The loudness practice places constraints on dialogue normalization, peak limit, and other parameters related to loudness. 
A setting of Custom allows the user to specify the values of parameters that would be set automatically if a loudness regulation were specified.

leveler_mode.description:
Specifies the loudness leveler mode appropriate for the audio content.
When the parameter is set to _auto_, the leveler follows the source metadata to apply loudness leveling for the regulation type.
When the parameter is set to _off_, the leveler operates in bypass mode (case of content already leveled). 
When the parameter is set to _on_, the leveler enables loudness leveling for the regulation type.

custom_properties.description:
For "Custom" Regulation Type, custom settings can be configured in this structure.
This is ignored for other Regulation Types.

peak_limit.description: Specifies the peak value to be used by the Dolby professional loudness correction library (dBTP)

dialogue_normalization.description: The level adjustment that a downstream decoder must perform to ensure that program dialogue is audible. Some loudness regulations impose specific values of dialnorm, which override any other setting specified by the user. (dB)

dialogue_intelligence.description: Enables or disables dialogue intelligence, a feature that identifies and analyzes dialogue segments within audio as a basis for speech gating.

from.title: Track configuration to use as parent.

line_mode_profile.title: Line mode profile
line_mode_profile.description: Indicates the dynamic range control profile (compression curve and related parameters) to use when playing back content in Line mode.

rf_mode_profile.title: RF mode profile
rf_mode_profile.description: Indicates the dynamic range control profile (compression curve and related parameters) to use when playing back content in RF mode.

loro_center_downmix_gain.title: Lo/Ro center downmix gain
loro_center_downmix_gain.description: Indicates the gain (in dB) that a decoder applies to the Center channel before adding it to the Left and Right channels while performing an Lo/Ro downmix.

loro_surround_downmix_gain.title: Lo/Ro surround downmix gain
loro_surround_downmix_gain.description: Indicates the gain (in dB) that a decoder applies to the surround channels before adding them to the Left and Right channels while performing an Lo/Ro downmix.

ltrt_center_downmix_gain.title: Lt/Rt center downmix gain
ltrt_center_downmix_gain.description: Indicates the gain (in dB) that a decoder applies to the Center channel before adding it to the Left and Right channels while performing an Lt/Rt downmix.

ltrt_surround_downmix_gain.title: Lt/Rt surround downmix gain
ltrt_surround_downmix_gain.description: Indicates the gain (in dB) that a decoder applies to the surround channels before adding them to the Left and Right channels while performing an Lt/Rt downmix.

preferred_stereo_downmix_mode.title: Preferred stereo downmix mode
preferred_stereo_downmix_mode.description: Indicates the method a decoder should use when downmixing the audio for playback.

surround_phase_shift.title: Surround phase shift
surround_phase_shift.description: Indicates whether a 90-degree phase-shift filter is applied to the surround channels to prevent cancellation in a subsequent surround-compatible downmix applied to the decoded signal.

correction_mode.description: Specifies whether the Dolby professional loudness correction library should modify the audio content or (alternatively) author metadata for a downstream decoder to use as the basis for leveling or correcting the audio.

regulation_type.description:
The Regulation Type specifies the loudness practice with which the leveled audio must comply.
Sometimes called loudness practice or target loudness regulation. 
The loudness practice places constraints on dialogue normalization, peak limit, and other parameters related to loudness. 
A setting of Custom allows the user to specify the values of parameters that would be set automatically if a loudness regulation were specified.

custom_properties.description:
For "Custom" Regulation Type, custom settings can be configured in this structure.
This is ignored for other Regulation Types.

peak_limit.description: Specifies the peak value to be used by the Dolby professional loudness correction library (dBTP)

dialogue_normalization.description: The level adjustment that a downstream decoder must perform to ensure that program dialogue is audible. Some loudness regulations impose specific values of dialnorm, which override any other setting specified by the user. (dB)

from.title: Track configuration to use as parent.

line_mode_profile.title: Line mode profile
line_mode_profile.description: Indicates the dynamic range control profile (compression curve and related parameters) to use when playing back content in Line mode.

rf_mode_profile.title: RF mode profile
rf_mode_profile.description: Indicates the dynamic range control profile (compression curve and related parameters) to use when playing back content in RF mode.

loro_center_downmix_gain.title: Lo/Ro center downmix gain
loro_center_downmix_gain.description: Indicates the gain (in dB) that a decoder applies to the Center channel before adding it to the Left and Right channels while performing an Lo/Ro downmix.

loro_surround_downmix_gain.title: Lo/Ro surround downmix gain
loro_surround_downmix_gain.description: Indicates the gain (in dB) that a decoder applies to the surround channels before adding them to the Left and Right channels while performing an Lo/Ro downmix.

ltrt_center_downmix_gain.title: Lt/Rt center downmix gain
ltrt_center_downmix_gain.description: Indicates the gain (in dB) that a decoder applies to the Center channel before adding it to the Left and Right channels while performing an Lt/Rt downmix.

ltrt_surround_downmix_gain.title: Lt/Rt surround downmix gain
ltrt_surround_downmix_gain.description: Indicates the gain (in dB) that a decoder applies to the surround channels before adding them to the Left and Right channels while performing an Lt/Rt downmix.

preferred_stereo_downmix_mode.title: Preferred stereo downmix mode
preferred_stereo_downmix_mode.description: Indicates the method a decoder should use when downmixing the audio for playback.

surround_phase_shift.title: Surround phase shift
surround_phase_shift.description: Indicates whether a 90-degree phase-shift filter is applied to the surround channels to prevent cancellation in a subsequent surround-compatible downmix applied to the decoded signal.

from.title: Track configuration to use as parent.

profile.title: Profile
profile.description: AAC encoding profile, between LC, HE and HEv2. High efficiency profiles are used for lower bitrates.

format.title: Format
format.description: AAC output bitstream format. Automatic choice is ADTS MPEG-2 for LC profile and ADTS MPEG-4 for HE profiles.

temporal_noise_shaping.title: Temporal Noise Shaping
temporal_noise_shaping.description: When enabled, the encoder is allowed (but not required) to use temporal noise shaping (TNS). This feature should only be disabled in case of compatibility issues.

midside_stereo.title: Mid/Side Stereo Optimisations
midside_stereo.description: When enabled, the encoder is allowed (but not required) to use mid/side stereo optimisations. This feature should only be disabled in case of compatibility issues.

_________common_definitions_audio_signal.title: MP4/fMP4 Audio Signaling
_________common_definitions_audio_signal.description:
Audio signaling in MP4/fMP4 outputs.

Important: MP4/fMP4 audio signaling is intended for audio tracks in MP4, DASH, CMAF, HLS fmp4 & fMP4 outputs only.

aac_profile.title: AAC Profile
aac_profile.description:
AAC codec profile signalized inside ISOBMFF boxes.

This signaling only applies for AAC tracks muxed inside MP4/fMP4 compliant outputs.

Must be one of:
* __implicit__: only base codec is signalized. Decoder may parse supplemental information.
* __explicit_hierarchical__: codec extensions are explicitly signalized. A decoder supporting only base codec 
will not manage to decode.
* __explicit_backward_compatible__ (default): base codec is signalized and extensions too

from.title: Track configuration to use as parent.

quality_speed.title: Quality-speed tradeoff
quality_speed.description:
Quality index to set the encoder speed.
The higher the quality index, the slower the encoding process.
20 is the fastest acceptable quality, when 80 is the better quality feasible.

profile.title: Profile
profile.description: VVC encoding profile. Only supported value is `main 10`.

level.title: Level
level.description: VVC encoding level. If set to _auto_, the encoder choose the least level compatible with the input video and encoder configuration.

_________common_definitions_video_denois.title: Denoising Parameters
_________common_definitions_video_denois.description: Denoiser processing parameters. Missing denoising parameter disables the denoising process.

type.title: Denoiser Type
type.description: Denoiser type. _mctf_ means motion compensated temporal filtering.

strength.title: Denoiser Strength
strength.description: Denoiser strength, from 1 to 10. Higher values imply stronger denoising.

_________common_definitions_video_sharpe.title: Sharpener Strength
_________common_definitions_video_sharpe.description: Sharpener strength, from 1 to 10. Higher values imply stronger sharpening. Zero or missing parameter disables the sharpening process.

_________common_definitions_video_hdr10p.title: HDR10+ processing
_________common_definitions_video_hdr10p.description:
HDR10+ processing computes SEI metadata that will be used to display HDR content on devices.
The only supported scene mode is "frame": it is the live mode to compute metadata.
More modes will be added later.

target_monitor_peak.title: Target Monitor Peak
target_monitor_peak.description: Maximum luminance in candels of the targeted monitor set in the Samsung SDK

_________common_definitions_video_allow_.title: Allow IDR Flag
_________common_definitions_video_allow_.description: Allow IDR (Instantaneous Decoder Refresh) frames to be freely inserted by the encoder. If set to false, I frames will be used instead. IDR frames can still be inserted by chunking decisions.

_________common_definitions_video_dither.title: Dithering Setting
_________common_definitions_video_dither.description: Dithering will be applied when input bit-depth is bigger than output one. Today `auto` mode is equivalent to `disabled`. In the near future, an automatic configuration will be selected depending on the use cases.

_________common_definitions_video_sdr_tr.title: SDR Transfer Characteristics
_________common_definitions_video_sdr_tr.description:
If this value is different than the one resulting from video colorimetry processing, this alternative value
will be set in VUI (i.e. backward SDR info), and the previous one will be stored in alternative transfer
characteristics SEI (i.e. HDR info).

_________common_definitions_video_open_g.title: Open Gop Flag
