_________common_definitions_video_open_g.description: Allow open GOP usage in the output stream. Some applications may want to disable open gop structures, at the price of more flickering near intra frames.

_________common_definitions_video_hdr_se.title: HDR SEI Metadata Flag
_________common_definitions_video_hdr_se.description:
Include HDR SEI metadata in the output bitstream.
This flag drives the presence of all (or none) of:
  - Alternative Transfer Characteristics
  - Light Level Information
  - Mastering Display Color Volume

Each of these are embedded in a separate SEI.

_________common_definitions_video_hrd_ya.title: HRD Flag
_________common_definitions_video_hrd_ya.description: Include HRD information in the output bitstream. HRD information is related to bitrate and CPB states.

_________common_definitions_video_afd_ya.title: AFD Flag
_________common_definitions_video_afd_ya.description: Include active format descriptor (AFD) related to aspect ratio in the encoded bitstream. Some decoders like to find this information in the encoded stream.

_________common_definitions_disable_slic.title: Disable Slices
_________common_definitions_disable_slic.description:
Enabling this parameter forces the encoder to use one slice per image.
Letting it disabled means allowing the encoder to choose the best number of slices for each frame

_________common_definitions_video_weight.title: Weighted Prediction Flag
_________common_definitions_video_weight.description: Controls the use of the weighted prediction tool. Weighted prediction should not be disabled except for compatibility issues with old decoders.

_________common_definitions_video_multi_.title: Multi Reference Flag
_________common_definitions_video_multi_.description: Controls the use of multiple references in the encoder. Multiple references should not be disabled except for compatibility issues with old decoders.

_________common_definitions_b_frames_yam.title: B Frame Configuration
_________common_definitions_b_frames_yam.description: Parameters related to B (bidirectional) frames. B frames are encoded out-of-order in order to reference past and future frames, improving the encoding quality.

min.title: Minimum Consecutive B Frames
min.description: Minimum number of consecutive B frames. Zero does not impose constraints on the frame type decision engine, thus provides the highest encoding quality.

max.title: Maximum Consecutive B Frames
max.description: Maximum number of consecutive B frames. Zero turns off the usage of B frames.

hierarchical.title: Hierarchical B Frames
hierarchical.description: This flag, if set to false, is used to turn off hierarchical (pyramidal) B frames, for compatibility issues. Disabling them reduces the encoding video quality.

anyOf.title: VMAF settings
anyOf.description: Additional settings for tuning the encoder specifically for VMAF algorithms

_________common_definitions_video_optimi.title: Psychovisual Setting
_________common_definitions_video_optimi.description: The encoder will tune its encoding decisions according to user preference. Option _psnr_ should be used when plotting encoding efficiency curves, otherwise _visual_ is the recommended setting.

text.title: Text
text.description: Text to be layered over the video. This text can contain ${TIMECODE} variable.

size.title: Font Size
size.description: Font Size of the text, in % of input video height or pixels. Height is limited to 100% of the input video height.

path.title: Font Path
path.description: Path of the font that will be applied to the text.

size.title: Background Size
size.description: Text background size. If not set, the background size is automatically adjusted to fit the text or set according to the size of the scrolling area.

oneOf.title: String Width
oneOf.description: Text type background width.

oneOf.title: Digital Width
oneOf.description: Relative type of background width as a percentage of video width.

oneOf.title: String Height
oneOf.description: Background height textual type.

oneOf.title: Digital Height
oneOf.description: Relative type of background height as a percentage of video height.

padding.title: Text Padding
padding.description: Extra margin between the text and the border of the bounding box.

oneOf.title: String Left Padding
oneOf.description: Left padding textual type.

oneOf.title: Digital Left Padding
oneOf.description: Relative type of left padding as a percentage of background width.

oneOf.title: String Top Padding
oneOf.description: Top padding textual type.

oneOf.title: Digital Top Padding
oneOf.description: Relative type of top padding as a percentage of background height.

oneOf.title: String Abscissa
oneOf.description: Textual kind of anchor abscissa.

oneOf.title: Digital Abscissa
oneOf.description: Relative kind of anchor abscissa in percent of video width.

oneOf.title: String Ordinate
oneOf.description: Textual kind of anchor ordinate.

oneOf.title: Digital Abscissa
oneOf.description: Relative kind of anchor ordinate in percent of video height.

horizontal.title: Horizontal
horizontal.description: Horizontal alignment (horizontal position of anchor relative to text).

vertical.title: Vertical
vertical.description: Vertical alignment (vertical position of anchor relative to text).

_________common_definitions_logos_yaml.title: Logos
_________common_definitions_logos_yaml.description:
List of logos to insert in the video.
Only PNG format is supported.

transparency.title: Logo transparency
transparency.description:
The minimal transparency value in percent that will be used when the logo will blends with the video frames.
This value can not be set to 100.0, as the logo would be entirely transparent.
Has no impact when set to 0.0.

top.title: Top
top.description: Logo top position, expressed as a percentage of the output video vertical resolution.

left.title: Left
left.description: Logo left position, expressed as a percentage of the output video horizontal resolution.

logo_size_reference.title: Size Reference
logo_size_reference.description: Indicates whether the logo _size_ parameter that follows is expressed with regards to the output video width or height.

size.title: Size
size.description: Logo blend size, expressed in percentage of the output video width or height, as defined by the _logo_size_reference_ parameter. If not set, the logo keeps its original pixel size.

display_aspect_ratio.title: Logo Display Aspect ratio
display_aspect_ratio.description: Logo display aspect ratio. If this field is not present, the logo will be assumed to have square pixels, hence have a display aspect ratio equal to its dimension. If the logo does not have square pixels, it will be shrunk or stretched to keep the display aspect ratio intact.

start.title: Display Start Time
start.description: Logo display start time, in seconds, where 0 is the first video frame.

end.title: Display End Time
end.description: Logo display end time, in seconds, where 0 is interpreted as display forever.

fade_in.title: Fade In
fade_in.description: Logo fade in duration, in seconds, where 0 means no fade in.

fade_out.title: Fade Out
fade_out.description: Logo fade out duration, in seconds, where 0 means no fade out.

_________common_definitions_video_colori.title: Colorimetry Conversion Parameters
_________common_definitions_video_colori.description: Colorimetry conversion processing parameters. Missing colorimetry conversion parameter node will results in default values usage for every following parameters.

oneOf.title: Colorimetry Type
oneOf.description:
Predefined sets of colorimetry parameters Color Primaries, Transfer Characteristics, and Matrix Coefficient
will be used as follow:
  - `disabled` prevents any colorimetry conversion (acts like pass-through)
  - `auto` will choose the value that fits the most the output characteristics (regarding frame size and frame rate)

| Enum      | Description      | Color Primaries | Transfer Characteristics | Matrix Coefficient |
| --------- | ---------------- | --------------- | ------------------------ | ------------------ |
| bt601_525 | ITU-R BT-601 525 | bt601           | bt601                    | bt601              |
| bt601_625 | ITU-R BT-601 625 | bt601           | bt601                    | bt601              |
| bt709     | ITU-R BT-709     | bt709           | bt709                    | bt709              |
| bt2020    | ITU-R BT-2020    | bt2020          | bt2020                   | bt2020             |
| st2084    | ITU-R BT-2100 PQ | bt2020          | st2084                   | bt2020             |
| aribb67   | ARIB STD-B67     | bt2020          | aribb67                  | bt2020             |

oneOf.title: Custom Colorimetry Conversion
oneOf.description: Color Primaries, Transfer Characteristics, and Matrix Coefficient parameters can be set independently. A custom 3D-LUT can be set for fine tuned processing, in this case `color_primaries` and `transfer_characteristics` set here are used for signaling purpose only.

color_primaries.title: Color Primaries Conversion
color_primaries.description:
Target color primaries:
- `pass-through` will use input signal information
- `auto` will choose the value that fits the most the output characteristics (regarding frame size and frame rate)

If `custom_lut` is used, this field will only be used for VUI signaling purpose.

transfer_characteristics.title: Transfer Characteristics Conversion
transfer_characteristics.description:
Target transfer characteristics:
- `pass-through` will use input signal information
- `auto` will choose the value that fits the most the output characteristics (regarding frame size and frame rate)

If `custom_lut` is used, this field will only be used for VUI signaling purpose.

matrix_coefficients.title: Matrix Coefficients Conversion
matrix_coefficients.description:
Target matrix coefficients:
- `pass-through` will use input signal information
- `auto` will choose the value that fits the most the output characteristics (regarding frame size and frame rate)

custom_lut.title: Custom LUT
custom_lut.description:
The string value may be either a file path or the LUT content.\
This 3D-LUT will squash processing steps defined by `color_primaries` and `transfer_characteristics`, then
these previous fields will only be used for output signaling purpose. `matrix_coefficients` remains used
by processing for conversion to output formats that are Color Difference Component Image (i.e. YUV)

video_range.title: Video Range Conversion
video_range.description:
Specify behaviour regarding video range:
- `pass-through` will prevent performing any conversion on the output
- `head` will convert output to head range if input was full range
- `full` will convert output to full range if input was head range

chroma_sample_location.title: Chroma Sample Location
chroma_sample_location.description: Specifies if any transformation must be performed on sub-sampled chromacities (i.e. YUV 4:2:0).

_________common_definitions_tone_mapping.title: Tone Mapping
_________common_definitions_tone_mapping.description:
Tone mapping occurs if converting between SDR and HDR is needed, specifies which tone mapping method is used. Else, 
does nothing. 
Supported methods are: 
  - eotf_scaling: use a modified range when applying inverse EOTF
  - normative: normatives conversion
  - hable: Hable tone mapping
  - bbc_normative: use BBC LUTs
  - bbc_upconvert: use BBC LUTs with up-conversion
  - eotf_scaling_movielabs: use BT.2408 recommended range when applying inverse EOTF (PQ only)

duration.title: CPB Duration
duration.description:
CPB duration, in seconds.
The CPB is an abstract object that controls the local bitrate variability.
Reducing it reduces the overall latency but reduces the video quality.

initial_occupancy.title: CPB Initial Occupancy
initial_occupancy.description: CPB initial occupancy, expressed as a percentage of its duration.

_________common_definitions_gop_size_yam.title: GOP size
_________common_definitions_gop_size_yam.description:
GOP size parameters.
GOP size is the distance between two intra (seekable) frames.
This distance can be fixed if the minimum and maximum gop sizes are both set to the same value.
However, doing so reduces the encoding quality near scene cuts as the encoder is not free to choose the intra frame location.

unit.title: GOP Unit
unit.description:
GOP unit which is frame or millisecond.
By default, the value of GOP unit is frame.

min.title: Minimum Size
min.description:
Minimum gop size, in number of frames or in milliseconds.
If this value is very high, it will be reduced to a more suitable value.

max.title: Maximum Size
max.description:
Maximum gop size, in number of frames or in milliseconds.
Zero is replaced by a suitable default value that depends on multiple other parameters.
If non zero, should be higher or equal to the minimum gop size.
If this value is very high, it will be reduced to a more suitable value.

_________common_definitions_video_high_t.title: High Tier Level
_________common_definitions_video_high_t.description: High tier level flag, for using higher bitrates.

_________common_definitions_deinterlacer.title: Deinterlacer
_________common_definitions_deinterlacer.description:
Deinterlacer implementation.
The deinterlacer is only used when the input video is interlaced and the requested output type is progressive:
- adaptive: Lightweight deinterlacer. Provides decent quality at very low CPU cost.
- smart_interpolation: Smart pixel-level interpolation algorithm, with an analysis of neighboring pixels. Provides a better quality, with an average CPU cost.
- advanced_fast: High quality motion compensated deinterlacer. Provides good quality at consequent CPU cost.
- advanced_best: Very high quality motion compensated neural network deinterlacer. Provides best quality, at highest CPU cost.

_________common_definitions_target_quali.description:
Target encoding quality, where 0 is the minimum quality and 100 the maximum.
Even if the requested quality is very high, the maximum bitrate allowed will not be exceeded.
The target quality is only used by constant quality rate control modes.
The following values describe the perceived quality:
  - 100: best possible quality
  - 90: Very high quality, transparent
  - 80: High quality, difficult to distinguish from source
  - 50: Decent quality
  - 30: Low quality, some artifacts
  - 10: Barely watchable, lots of artifacts
  - 5: Not watchable
  - 0: Worst possible quality

_________common_definitions_bitrate_max_.title: Maximum Bitrate (in bits per second)
_________common_definitions_bitrate_max_.description:
Maximum allowed bitrate, in bits/s, where 0 is replaced by the maximum value allowed by the profile and level parameters.
Very low values will be increased by a sustainable minimum by the encoder.
In any case, the value must be greater or equal than the target bitrate.
The bitrate max is only used by `constant quality` and `variable bitrate` rate control modes.

_________common_definitions_bitrate_yaml.title: Bitrate (target in bits per second)
_________common_definitions_bitrate_yaml.description:
Target bitrate, in bits/s.
Very low values will be increased to a sustainable minimum by the encoder.
The maximum value depends on the profile and level configuration.
The target bitrate is only used by `constant bitrate` and `variable bitrate` rate control modes.

_________common_definitions_rate_control.title: Rate Control Mode
_________common_definitions_rate_control.description:
Rate control mode.
- `constant bitrate` produces constant bitrate stream with a single pass video transcoding.
    The parameter to configure with this mode is `bitrate` in the encoder configuration
- `constant quality` produces constant video quality stream with a single pass video transcoding.
    The parameters to configure with this mode are `target_quality` and `bitrate_max` in the encoder configuration
- `variable bitrate` produces high video quality stream with a two passes video encoding:
    - The 1st video pass gathers statistics (on the coding complexity) used for the 2nd one.
    - The 2nd video pass produces optimal video quality stream under average bitrate constraint.
    The parameters to configure with this mode are `bitrate` and `bitrate_max` in the encoder configuration.

_________common_definitions_cropping_yam.title: Cropping Parameters
_________common_definitions_cropping_yam.description:
Cropping configuration, with regards to input video resolution.
If this field is not present, input video is not cropped.

oneOf.title: Automatic Cropping Type
oneOf.description:
Predefined sets of cropping rules.
  - `black_bars` will remove all outer black bars to keep the non-black area of the input video
  - `horizontal_black_bars` will only remove horizontal outer black bars
  - `vertical_black_bars` will only remove vertical outer black bars

oneOf.title: Custom Cropping Configuration
oneOf.description: Manual cropping parameters.

top.title: Top
top.description: Crop top Y position, in pixels.

left.title: Left
left.description: Crop left X position, in pixels.

conditions.title: Cropping conditions
conditions.description:
Crop video if input video resolution matches the cropping conditions. In case of concatenation, this condition
is applied to each segment with regards to its input resolution.

_________common_definitions_frame_rate_r.title: Frame Rate Resampling Type
_________common_definitions_frame_rate_r.description:
In order to configure a frame rate resampling type, you can choose between two methods:
* `duplication`: frames are simply dropped or duplicated
* `interpolation`: frames are temporally interpolated

oneOf.description: The first element in the array is the frame rate numerator and the second element is the denominator.

_________common_definitions_frame_resize.title: Resize Type
_________common_definitions_frame_resize.description: Defines the resize algorithm used internally. Has an impact on the visual impression (sharpness, ringing) of the input video. This parameter is ignored if a frame resolution has not been provided.

_________common_definitions_frame_size_y.title: Frame Size
_________common_definitions_frame_size_y.description:
In order to set the size you can configure:
* A predefined value `pass-through` so the size of the frames after cropping will be preserved.
* Both of __width__ and __height__ so the size of your frames will be what you configure
* Only __width__ so the height will be automatically computed to respect the aspect ratios (DAR and PAR) after cropping
* Only __height__ so the width will be automatically computed to respect the aspect ratios (DAR and PAR) after cropping

width.title: Frame width
width.description: Sets the output frame width, in pixels

height.title: Frame height
height.description: Sets the output frame height, in pixels

from.title: Track configuration to use as parent.
Track configuration to use as parent.
Track configuration to use as parent.

profile.title: Profile
profile.description: Prores encoding profile.

_________common_definitions_video_colori.title: Colorimetry Conversion Parameters
_________common_definitions_video_colori.description: Colorimetry conversion processing parameters. Missing colorimetry conversion parameter node will result in default values usage for every following parameters.

oneOf.title: Colorimetry Type
oneOf.description:
Predefined set of colorimetry parameters Color Primaries, Transfer Characteristics, and Matrix Coefficient
will be used as follow:
  - `disabled` prevents any colorimetry conversion (acting like pass-through)
  - `auto` will choose the value that fits the most the output characteristics (regarding frame size and frame rate)

| Enum      | Description      | Color Primaries | Transfer Characteristics | Matrix Coefficient |
| --------- | ---------------- | --------------- | ------------------------ | ------------------ |
| bt601_525 | ITU-R BT-601 525 | bt601           | bt601                    | bt601              |
| bt601_625 | ITU-R BT-601 625 | bt601           | bt601                    | bt601              |
| bt709     | ITU-R BT-709     | bt709           | bt709                    | bt709              |
| bt2020    | ITU-R BT-2020    | bt2020          | bt2020                   | bt2020             |
| st2084    | ITU-R BT-2100 PQ | bt2020          | st2084                   | bt2020             |
| aribb67   | ARIB STD-B67     | bt2020          | aribb67                  | bt2020             |

oneOf.title: Custom Colorimetry Conversion
oneOf.description: Color Primaries, Transfer Characteristics, and Matrix Coefficient parameters can be set independently. A custom 3D-LUT can be set for fine tuned processing, in this case `color_primaries` and `transfer_characteristics` set here are used for signaling purpose only.

color_primaries.title: Color Primaries Conversion
color_primaries.description:
Target color primaries:
- `pass-through` will use input signal information
- `auto` will choose the value that fits the most the output characteristics (regarding frame size and frame rate)

If `custom_lut` is used, this field will only be used for VUI signaling purpose.

transfer_characteristics.title: Transfer Characteristics Conversion
transfer_characteristics.description:
Target transfer characteristics:
- `pass-through` will use input signal information
- `auto` will choose the value that fits the most the output characteristics (regarding frame size and frame rate)

If `custom_lut` is used, this field will only be used for VUI signaling purpose.

matrix_coefficients.title: Matrix Coefficients Conversion
matrix_coefficients.description:
Target matrix coefficients:
- `pass-through` will use input signal information
- `auto` will choose the value that fits the most the output characteristics (regarding frame size and frame rate)

custom_lut.title: Custom LUT
custom_lut.description:
The string value may be either a file path or the LUT content.\
This 3D-LUT will squash processing steps defined by `color_primaries` and `transfer_characteristics`, then
these previous fields will only be used for output signaling purpose. `matrix_coefficients` remains used
by processing for conversion to output formats that are Color Difference Component Image (i.e. YUV)

video_range.title: Video Range Conversion
video_range.description:
Specify behaviour regarding video range:
- `pass-through` will prevent performing any conversion on the output
- `head` will convert output to head range if input was full range
- `full` will convert output to full range if input was head range

_________common_definitions_m2v_config_x.title: MPEG2 configuration for XDCAM IMX conformance

from.title: Track configuration to use as parent.

_________common_definitions_quality_spee.title: Quality-Speed tradeoff
_________common_definitions_quality_spee.description: This field is used to set the encoder speed. The ultrafast option is specific to the HEVC codec. For other codecs ultrafast=fastest.

_________common_definitions_m2v_config_x.title: MPEG2 configuration for XDCAM HD conformance

Track configuration to use as parent.

profile.title: Profile
profile.description: MPEG-2 encoding profile. Bit depth is 8bits only.

level.title: Level
level.description: MPEG-2 encoding level. Levels _low_ and _high1440_ can only be used with _main_ profile, and level _high_ may not be used with _simple_ profile

encoding_mode.title: Encoding Mode
encoding_mode.description: The encoding mode defines the underlying frame/field structure in case of interlaced content.

b_frames.title: B Frame Configuration
b_frames.description: Parameters related to B (bidirectional) frames. B frames are encoded out-of-order in order to reference past and future frames, improving the encoding quality.

min.title: Minimum Consecutive B Frames
min.description: Minimum number of consecutive B frames. Zero does not impose constraints on the frame type decision engine, thus provides the highest encoding quality.

max.title: Maximum Consecutive B Frames
max.description: Maximum number of consecutive B frames. Zero turns off the usage of B frames.

from.title: Track configuration to use as parent.

profile.title: Profile
profile.description:
AV1 encoding profile.

| Enum                | Chroma subsampling | Bit depth |
| ------------------- | ------------------ | --------- |
| main                | 4:2:0              | 8         |
| main 10             | 4:2:0              | 10        |
| high                | 4:2:0              | 8         |
| high 10             | 4:2:0              | 10        |
| professional        | 4:2:0              | 8         |
| professional 422    | 4:2:2              | 8         |
| professional 10     | 4:2:0              | 10        |
| professional 422 10 | 4:2:2              | 10        |
| professional 12     | 4:2:0              | 12        |
| professional 422 12 | 4:2:2              | 12        |

level.title: Level
level.description: AV1 encoding level. If set to _auto_, the encoder choose the least level compatible with the input video and encoder configuration.

oneOf.title: Film Grain Synthesis Setting
oneOf.description: This option will guarantee the preservation of the grain after encoding.

_________common_definitions_video_debloc.description: Controls the use of the deblocking tool. Deblocking should not be disabled except for compatibility issues with old decoders.

oneOf.description: The deblocking offset is used to slightly increase (+6) or decrease (-6) the deblocking strength.

_________common_definitions_video_colori.title: Colorimetry Conversion Parameters
_________common_definitions_video_colori.description: Colorimetry conversion processing parameters. Missing colorimetry conversion parameter node will results in default values usage for every following parameters.

oneOf.title: Colorimetry Type
oneOf.description:
Predefined sets of colorimetry parameters Color Primaries, Transfer Characteristics, and Matrix Coefficient
will be used as follow:
  - `disabled` prevents any colorimetry conversion (acts like pass-through)
  - `auto` will choose the value that fits the most the output characteristics (regarding frame size and frame rate)

| Enum      | Description      | Color Primaries | Transfer Characteristics | Matrix Coefficient |
| --------- | ---------------- | --------------- | ------------------------ | ------------------ |
| bt601_525 | ITU-R BT-601 525 | bt601           | bt601                    | bt601              |
| bt601_625 | ITU-R BT-601 625 | bt601           | bt601                    | bt601              |
| bt709     | ITU-R BT-709     | bt709           | bt709                    | bt709              |
| bt2020    | ITU-R BT-2020    | bt2020          | bt2020                   | bt2020             |
| st2084    | ITU-R BT-2100 PQ | bt2020          | st2084                   | bt2020             |
| aribb67   | ARIB STD-B67     | bt2020          | aribb67                  | bt2020             |

oneOf.title: Custom Colorimetry Conversion
oneOf.description: Color Primaries, Transfer Characteristics, and Matrix Coefficient parameters can be set independently. A custom 3D-LUT can be set for fine tuned processing, in this case `color_primaries` and `transfer_characteristics` set here are used for signaling purpose only.

color_primaries.title: Color Primaries Conversion
color_primaries.description:
Target color primaries:
- `pass-through` will use input signal information
- `auto` will choose the value that fits the most the output characteristics (regarding frame size and frame rate)

If `custom_lut` is used, this field will only be used for VUI signaling purpose.

transfer_characteristics.title: Transfer Characteristics Conversion
transfer_characteristics.description:
Target transfer characteristics:
- `pass-through` will use input signal information
- `auto` will choose the value that fits the most the output characteristics (regarding frame size and frame rate)

If `custom_lut` is used, this field will only be used for VUI signaling purpose.

matrix_coefficients.title: Matrix Coefficients Conversion
matrix_coefficients.description:
Target matrix coefficients:
- `pass-through` will use input signal information
- `auto` will choose the value that fits the most the output characteristics (regarding frame size and frame rate)

custom_lut.title: Custom LUT
custom_lut.description:
The string value may be either a file path or the LUT content.\
This 3D-LUT will squash processing steps defined by `color_primaries` and `transfer_characteristics`, then
these previous fields will only be used for output signaling purpose. `matrix_coefficients` remains used
by processing for conversion to output formats that are Color Difference Component Image (i.e. YUV)

video_range.title: Video Range Conversion
video_range.description:
Specify behaviour regarding video range:
- `pass-through` will prevent performing any conversion on the output
- `head` will convert output to head range if input was full range
- `full` will convert output to full range if input was head range

chroma_sample_position.title: Chroma Sample Position
chroma_sample_position.description: Specifies if any transformation must be performed on sub-sampled chromacities (i.e. YUV 4:2:0).

rate_control.title: Rate Control Mode
rate_control.description:
Rate control mode.
- `constant quality` produces constant video quality stream with a single pass video transcoding.
    The parameters to configure with this mode are `target_quality` and `bitrate_max` in the encoder configuration

bitrate_max.title: Maximum Bitrate (in bits per second)
bitrate_max.description:
Maximum allowed bitrate, in bits/s, where 0 is replaced by the maximum value allowed by the profile and level parameters.
Very low values will be increased by a sustainable minimum by the encoder.
In any case, the value must be greater or equal than the target bitrate.
The bitrate max is only used by `constant quality` and `variable bitrate` rate control modes.
Note that this value will be applied to both left and right views independently, so the final MV-HEVC stream
will not be constrained by a specified `max_bitrate` value. It would however be capped by twice this value.

_________common_definitions_multiview_si.title: Multiview Signaling
_________common_definitions_multiview_si.description: Multiview Video Signaling. Describes the content of "vexu" boxes inserted in MP4 / fMP4 outputs.

stereo_view_information.title: Stereo View Information
stereo_view_information.description: Stereo view information box (`stri`) carrying the stereography related information.

eye_views_reversed.title: Eye Views Reversed
eye_views_reversed.description: This flag indicates whether the stereo eye views are reversed from the default order of left eye being first and right eye being second.

has_additional_views.title: Additional Views Presence
has_additional_views.description: This flag indicates whether one or more additional views may be present beyond stereo left and stereo right eyes (e.g,. a "centerline" view)

has_right_eye_view.title: Right Eye View Presence
has_right_eye_view.description: This flag indicates whether the stereo right eye is present in video frames.

has_left_eye_view.title: Left Eye View Presence
has_left_eye_view.description: This flag indicates whether the stereo left eye is present in video frames.

hero_eye_indicator.title: Hero Eye indicator
hero_eye_indicator.description:
Indicates the hero eye as stored in the `hero`box:
  - 0: None
  - 1: Left stero eye
  - 2: Right stereo eye
  - >2: Reserved values

spatial_signaling.title: Spatial Signaling
spatial_signaling.description: Spatial video signaling. If enabled, all values related to spatial signaling below will be inserted.

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: This flag indicates whether spatial video signaling boxes shall be inserted.

camera_distance.title: Camera Distance
camera_distance.description: Specifies the distance, in micrometers, between centers of the lenses of the camera system. This controls the value in the stereo camera baseline box (`blin`).

horizontal_adjustment.title: Horizontal Adjustment
horizontal_adjustment.description: Horizontal disparity adjustment indicating a relative shift of the left and right images, which changes the zero parallax plane. This controls the value in the disparity adjustment box (`dadj`).

projection.title: Projection
projection.description: Projection format FourCC as follows: - `rect`: rectangular (standard spatial video) - `equi`: equirectangular (360-degree immersive video) - `hequ`: half-equirectangular (180-degree immersive video) - `fish`: fisheye (fisheye immersive video) This controls the value in the projection information box (`prji`).

horizontal_field_of_view.title: Horizontal Field Of View
horizontal_field_of_view.description: Horizontal field of view box, in degrees. This controls the value in the horizontal field of view box (`hfov`).

_________common_definitions_b_frames_wit.title: B Frame Configuration
_________common_definitions_b_frames_wit.description: Parameters related to B (bidirectional) frames. B frames are encoded out-of-order in order to reference past and future frames, improving the encoding quality.

min.title: Minimum Consecutive B Frames
min.description: Minimum number of consecutive B frames. Zero does not impose constraints on the frame type decision engine, thus provides the highest encoding quality.

max.title: Maximum Consecutive B Frames
max.description: Maximum number of consecutive B frames. Zero turns off the usage of B frames.

_________common_definitions_frame_size_d.title: Frame Size Dolby Vision
_________common_definitions_frame_size_d.description:
The frame size can be either defined by an array of two integers representing the width and the height of the
frame (ex: [1920, 1080]) or set to one of the frame size predefined by Dolby.

width.title: Frame width
width.description: Sets the output frame width, in pixels

height.title: Frame height
height.description: Sets the output frame height, in pixels

rate_control.title: Rate Control Mode
rate_control.description:
Rate control mode.
- `constant bitrate` produces constant bitrate stream with a single pass video transcoding.
    The parameter to configure with this mode is `bitrate` in the encoder configuration.
- `constant quality` produces constant video quality stream with a single pass video transcoding.
    The parameters to configure with this mode are `target_quality` and `bitrate_max` in the encoder configuration
- `variable bitrate` produces high video quality stream with a two passes video encoding:
    - The 1st video pass gathers statistics (on the coding complexity) used for the 2nd one.
    - The 2nd video pass produces optimal video quality stream under average bitrate constraint.
    The parameters to configure with this mode are `bitrate` and `bitrate_max` in the encoder configuration.

bitrate.title: Bitrate (target in bits per second)
bitrate.description:
Target bitrate, in bits/s.
Very low values will be increased to a sustainable minimum by the encoder.
The maximum value depends on the profile and level configuration.
The target bitrate is not used by constant quality rate control modes.

bitrate_max.title: Maximum Bitrate (in bits per second)
bitrate_max.description:
Maximum allowed bitrate, in bits/s, where 0 is replaced by the maximum value allowed by the profile and level parameters.
Very low values will be increased by a sustainable minimum by the encoder.
In any case, the value must be greater or equal (for the constant bitrate case) than the target bitrate.
This value applies to all rate control modes.

profile.title: Profile
profile.description: Dolby Vision encoding profile. This will decide most of the output parameters (bit-depth, HDR settings, etc.). Profile 5 is single layer and has no backward compatibility. Profile 8.1 is single layer and is compatible with HDR10 signal.  Profile 8.1 has another variant using a dynamic content mapping up to 1000 nits for its compatible signal.

metadata_postprocessing.description: Settings specific to the Dolby Vision metadata postprocessor

disable_l4_update.title: Disables L4 Metadata RPU Update
disable_l4_update.description: Prevents Postprocessor from updating Dolby Vision L4 values.

disable_l6_update.title: Disables L6 Metadata RPU Update
disable_l6_update.description: Prevents Postprocessor from updating Dolby Vision L6 values.

disable_sei_update.title: Disables HDR10 SEI Update
disable_sei_update.description: Prevents Postprocessor from updating HDR10 Content Light Level information.

override_l6.title: Overrides L6 Metadata RPU
override_l6.description: If present, will cause L6 values (Dolby Vision equivalent for HDR10 Content Light Level values) to be overriden.

maximum_content_light_level.title: Maximum Content Light Level
maximum_content_light_level.description: New value for the Maximum Content Light Level value in L6 Dolby Vision metadata.

maximum_frame_average_light_level.title: Maximum Frame Average Light Level
maximum_frame_average_light_level.description: New value for the Maximum Frame Average Light Level value in L6 Dolby Vision metadata.

override_sei.title: Overrides HDR10 SEI
override_sei.description: If present, will cause HDR10 SEI Content Light Level values to be overriden.

maximum_content_light_level.title: Maximum Content Light Level
maximum_content_light_level.description: New value for the Maximum Content Light Level value in HDR10 SEI.

maximum_frame_average_light_level.title: Maximum Frame Average Light Level
maximum_frame_average_light_level.description: New value for the Maximum Frame Average Light Level value in HDR10 SEI.

from.title: Track configuration to use as parent.

rate_control.title: Rate Control Mode
rate_control.description:
Rate control mode.
- `constant quality` produces constant video quality stream with a single pass video transcoding.
    The parameters to configure with this mode are `target_quality` and `bitrate_max` in the encoder configuration

bitrate_max.title: Maximum Bitrate (in bits per second)
bitrate_max.description:
Maximum allowed bitrate, in bits/s, where 0 is replaced by the maximum value allowed by the profile and level parameters.
Very low values will be increased by a sustainable minimum by the encoder.
In any case, the value must be greater or equal than the target bitrate.
The bitrate max is only used by `constant quality` and `variable bitrate` rate control modes.
Note that this value will be applied to both left and right views independently, so the final MV-HEVC stream
will not be constrained by a specified `max_bitrate` value. It would however be capped by twice this value.

profile.title: Profile
profile.description: HEVC encoding profile.

level.title: Level
level.description: HEVC encoding level. If set to _auto_, the encoder chooses the least level compatible with the input video and encoder configuration.

encoding_mode.title: Encoding Mode
encoding_mode.description: The encoding mode defines the underlying frame/field structure in case of interlaced content. If the encoding_mode is set to _auto_, the encoder picks up one of the possible mode. If a specific encoding mode is required, do not rely on the _auto_ parameter, as the default choice can change without notice between versions.

_________common_definitions_video_multip.title: Multiple PPS/SPS
_________common_definitions_video_multip.description: The encoder may use more than one picture parameter set, unless this flag is disabled. Disabling it has a mild impact on video encoding quality but is required for some poorly coded packagers.

_________common_definitions_custom_matri.title: Custom Matrices
_________common_definitions_custom_matri.description: Controls the use of custom matrices.

from.title: Track configuration to use as parent.

profile.title: Profile
profile.description: HEVC encoding profile.

level.title: Level
level.description: HEVC encoding level. If set to _auto_, the encoder choose the least level compatible with the input video and encoder configuration.

encoding_mode.title: Encoding Mode
encoding_mode.description: The encoding mode defines the underlying frame/field structure in case of interlaced content. If the encoding_mode is set to _auto_, the encoder picks up one of the possible mode. If a specific encoding mode is required, do not rely on the _auto_ parameter, as the default choice can change without notice between versions.

_________common_definitions_avc_config_i.title: AVC configuration for Sony XAVC Intra

from.title: Track configuration to use as parent.

_________common_definitions_avc_config_i.title: AVC configuration for Panasonic AVC Intra

from.title: Track configuration to use as parent.
Track configuration to use as parent.

profile.title: Profile
profile.description: AVC encoding profile.

level.title: Level
level.description: AVC encoding level. If set to _auto_, the encoder choose the least level compatible with the input video and encoder configuration.

_________common_definitions_video_entrop.title: Entropy Coding Mode
_________common_definitions_video_entrop.description: Selection between CABAC and CAVLC entry coding modes. Using CAVLC increases the encoding bitrate by at least 10%. CAVLC should only be used for very high bitrates when there are performance issues with decoders.

_________common_definitions_video_encodi.title: Encoding Mode
_________common_definitions_video_encodi.description: The encoding mode defines the underlying frame/field structure in case of interlaced content.

_________common_definitions_subtitles_ch.title: Subtitles Chunk Period
_________common_definitions_subtitles_ch.description:
Nominal subtitles chunk period. This parameter is only applied for OTT and Elementary Stream outputs. 2 configuration modes are possible:
- disabled: The subtitles chunk period is disabled.
- a number of seconds

_________common_definitions_timeline_ref.title: Timeline Reference
_________common_definitions_timeline_ref.description:
Indicates whether the trimming timecode matches actual timecodes from the inputs or not.

If set to `zero_based`, the asset timeline will be assumed to start with a 00:00:00:00 timecode and the trimming will be applied relatively to this."
If set to `source`, the trimming will be done according to timecodes extracted from the asset level, or from the video level if nothing is detected in the assets.          

Please note that if no timecode can be extracted from the asset or video, 00:00:00:00 will be used by default.

_________common_definitions_time_indicat.title: Time Indication
_________common_definitions_time_indicat.description: Time indication, either as a timestamp, a frame number or a timecode. Used for start and end times for segment definitions.

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description:
Representation of a timestamp value, in seconds. Type timestamp can also have negative values. For the trimming 
use case it will be interpreted as counted from the end of the inputs. The end of the inputs is defined as the 
maximum duration among all assets involved in this segment. For instance, with a maximum asset duration of 90 frames, 
the value "-5 timestamps" is strictly equivalent to the maximum asset duration from which the frame-converted value 
of "-5 timestamps" must be subtracted.

frame.title: Frame
frame.description:
Value indicating the number of frames. Type frame can also have negative values. For the trimming use case it will 
be interpreted as counted from the end of the inputs. The end of the inputs is defined as the 
maximum duration among all assets involved in this segment. For instance, with a maximum asset duration of 90 frames, 
the value "-5 frames" is strictly equivalent to the value "85 frames"

_________common_definitions_input_data_f.title: Input data filters
_________common_definitions_input_data_f.description:
Each field of this object must be a stream selection filter that will be use to identify tracks in asset.
To be selected an Elementary Stream from an asset must match all the selection filters.

_________common_definitions_program_posi.title: Program position
_________common_definitions_program_posi.description: Program position filter. The stream is selected if the position of the program it belongs to, among all the programs from the asset, matches this value. Most asset formats are limited to a single program, so this filter will only be useful for multiple-programs format (e.g. MPEG TS). Note that you cannot simultaneously use different programs from the same asset. If this field is not present, stream selection will target the same program as a previously mapped stream from this asset, or the first program if there is none.

_________common_definitions_stream_posit.title: Stream position
_________common_definitions_stream_posit.description: Stream position filter. The stream is selected if its position, among all the tracks of the same type from the asset, matches this value. If the asset has multiple programs (e.g. MPEG TS), the ordering will be limited to tracks in a single program. In this case, using the program_position filter is recommended. If this field is not present, stream selection will target the stream with the lowest position that hasn't already been selected.

_________common_definitions_data_codec_f.title: Data Codec
_________common_definitions_data_codec_f.description:
Data stream type.
The stream is selected if it is a data stream of the provided type.
If this field is not present, the data stream type is not taken into account for stream selection.
Stream types are interpreted as follows:
  - __nielsen_watermarking__: Nielsen watermarking signal extracted from an audio source
  - __id3_nielsen__: ID3 track carrying data related to Nielsen watermarking
  - __id3_custom__: ID3 track carrying any other kind of data

_________common_definitions_mxf_index_fi.title: Index
_________common_definitions_mxf_index_fi.description: MXF track index. The stream is selected if its track index matches the one provided. If this field is not present, the track index is not taken into account for stream selection.

_________common_definitions_mp4_id_filte.title: Id
_________common_definitions_mp4_id_filte.description: MP4/MOV track id. The stream is selected if its track id matches the one provided. If this field is not present, the track id is not taken into account for stream selection.

_________common_definitions_pid_filter_y.title: PID
_________common_definitions_pid_filter_y.description:
MPEG TS PID.
The stream is selected if its PID matches the one provided.
If this field is not present, the PID is not taken into account for stream selection.

_________common_definitions_input_subtit.title: Input subtitles text recognition
_________common_definitions_input_subtit.description:
Text recognition is used in the case where the asset source is an image subtitle
and the output uses an encoder that takes a subtitle as input text.
Currently the only supported image input is DVB subtitle
and the encoders that take text subtitles as input are: 

  ttml, webvtt, closed-caption, teletext, burned subtitle, dvb_subtitle(non passthrough).

To recognize the text, Titan File uses a Tesseract dictionary in the form of a directory of language data files.

Titan File includes a default dictionary with:

  English (eng), Spanish (spa), French (fre), German (ger) and Portuguese (por).

To use embedded dictionaries you have to leave the field dictionary_folder empty.

A custom dictionary can also be used for embedded languages if the subtitles use a more complex font
 and require a better trained dictionary for that type of font.

For any information on dictionaries, their construction or for the recovery of a more complete dictionary
it is necessary to refer to the tesseract documentation https://tesseract-ocr.github.io/tessdoc/Data-Files.html 

The embedded languages are taken from the tessdata-best dictionary.

language.title: Language
language.description:
Text language to be recognized.
Eng, spa, fre, ger and por are already included.

For the other languages, the dictionary must be provided or 
to override embedded languages for recognition customization (e.g. to recognize custom fonts).

The "auto" means that the language signaled in the source is used.
If it is not present, not supported or requires providing a custom dictionary, an error will be reported.

dictionary_folder.title: Dictionary folder URL
dictionary_folder.description:
Path to the folder that contains languages trained data files for text recognition.
Leave it unset to use trained data files that are embedded in the Titan File (eng, spa, fre, ger and por).

_________common_definitions_input_subtit.title: Input subtitles filters
_________common_definitions_input_subtit.description:
Each field of this object must be a stream selection filter that will be use to identify tracks in asset.
To be selected an Elementary Stream from an asset must match all the selection filters.

closed_captions_channel.title: Closed Caption channel
closed_captions_channel.description: Closed Caption channel to decode, when using non-passthrough closed caption input.

source_type.title: Subtitle input type
source_type.description:
Filters the format of input used, in order to adapt the subtitle workflow.
Used for cases when the actual subtitle origin is ambiguous when based on asset type/filters alone.
The following modes are supported:
  * Separate track, i.e. the subtitles have their own track in the asset and are not extracted from a video
  * Embedded in video track, i.e. the subtitles are extracted from the video layer (from the SEI or equivalent)

_________common_definitions_subtitle_typ.title: Subtitle Filter type
_________common_definitions_subtitle_typ.description:
Subtitle filter type. This type of filter can be used for both DVB teletext and DVB subtitles.
Each of these values is associated to a specific value in the descriptor.
For DVB Subtitles:
  a. 'normal' value covers the following types: 
      - DVB subtitles (normal) with no monitor aspect ratio criticality (subtitling_type 0x10 in the subtitling_descriptor)
      - DVB subtitles (normal) for display on 4:3 aspect ratio monitor (subtitling_type 0x11 in the subtitling_descriptor) 
      - DVB subtitles (normal) for display on 16:9 aspect ratio monitor (subtitling_type 0x12 in the subtitling_descriptor)
      - DVB subtitles (normal) for display on 2.21:1 aspect ratio monitor (subtitling_type 0x13 in the subtitling_descriptor)
      - DVB subtitles (normal) for display on high definition monitor (subtitling_type 0x14 in the subtitling_descriptor)
  b. 'hard_of_hearing' value covers the following types: 
      - DVB subtitles (for the hard of hearing) with no monitor aspect ratio criticality (subtitling_type 0x20 in the subtitling_descriptor)
      - DVB subtitles (for the hard of hearing) for display on 4:3 aspect ratio monitor (subtitling_type 0x21 in the subtitling_descriptor)
      - DVB subtitles (for the hard of hearing) for display on 16:9 aspect ratio monitor (subtitling_type 0x22 in the subtitling_descriptor)
      - DVB subtitles (for the hard of hearing) for display on 2.21:1 aspect ratio monitor (subtitling_type 0x23 in the subtitling_descriptor)
      - DVB subtitles (for the hard of hearing) for display on HD monitor (subtitling_type 0x24 in the subtitling_descriptor)

For DVB Teletext: 
  a. 'normal' value covers the following type: 
      - Teletext Subtitle (teletext_type 0x02 in the teletext descriptor)
  b. 'hard_of_hearing' value covers the following type:
      - Teletext subtitle page for hearing impaired people (teletext_type 0x05 in the teletext descriptor)

_________common_definitions_language_fil.title: Language
_________common_definitions_language_fil.description: Audio or subtitle stream language, as an ISO 639-2 three-letter code. The stream is selected if it has a language descriptor that matches the provided language code. If this field is not present, the stream language is not taken into account for stream selection.

_________common_definitions_subtitles_co.title: Subtitle Codec
_________common_definitions_subtitles_co.description: Subtitle stream type. The stream is selected if it is a subtitle stream of the provided type. If this field is not present, the subtitle format is not taken into account for stream selection.

_________common_definitions_input_audio_.title: Audio Overrides
_________common_definitions_input_audio_.description:
Audio parameters can be signaled in input file. These can be overridden thanks to following parameters.
Unlike overrides at track level, the values here will impact how the input signal is processed.

_________common_definitions_input_overri.title: Layout Override
_________common_definitions_input_overri.description: Overrides layout, as an array of channel position.

_________common_definitions_input_audio_.title: Input audio filters
_________common_definitions_input_audio_.description:
Each field of this object must be a stream selection filter that will be use to identify tracks in asset.
To be selected an Elementary Stream from an asset must match all the selection filters.

_________common_definitions_audio_dolby_.title: Audio Dolby Bitstream Mode
_________common_definitions_audio_dolby_.description: Lists the Dolby bitstream mode metadata filters. 'reserved' value is used for either voice over (VO) or karaoke modes.

_________common_definitions_audio_type_y.title: Audio Type
_________common_definitions_audio_type_y.description: Audio Type filter parameter values associated to the ISO 639 language descriptor.

_________common_definitions_audio_channe.title: Audio channel
_________common_definitions_audio_channe.description:
Audio channel list.
This field allows to select which channel to use for this stream, and in which order to use them.
Channels are specified using their input index (starting from 1) or their detected label.
The reordering is done *after* the channel layout overrides (if any).
e.g. if you use a ["front_left", "front_right"] override, a [2, 1] value will always yield channels labelled R then L.
Similarly with this override, a ["front_right", "front_left"], ["front_right, 1] or [2, "front_left"] will behave the same.

_________common_definitions_audio_progra.title: Audio Program Index
_________common_definitions_audio_progra.description: Audio program index in case of multi-program streams like Dolby-E or dual mono streams.

_________common_definitions_audio_codec_.title: Audio Codec
_________common_definitions_audio_codec_.description: Audio stream encoding standard. The stream is selected if it is an audio stream encoded with the provided codec. If this field is not present, the audio compression standard is not taken into account for stream selection. Value _m2a_ is kept for backward compatibility.

_________common_definitions_audio_compos.title: Audio composition index
_________common_definitions_audio_compos.description:
Audio composition index.
This field allows to select a specific stream when an asset uses audio composition.
If an asset uses a composition of 2+6+2, a composition index of 2 allows to target the 6-channel stream.
If the asset does not use composition, this field is considered as absent.

_________common_definitions_input_video_.title: Video Overrides
_________common_definitions_input_video_.description: Video parameters may be extracted from input files. Those parameters can be overriden with the following parameters.

frame_rate.title: Frame Rate Parameters Override
frame_rate.description:
Overrides the frame rate of the input video, thus changing the input duration without changing the number of frames.
The new frame rate must be at most 5% higher than the input frame rate. It cannot be lower than the input frame rate.
Example: for a frame rate of N fps, the maximum frame rate allowed will be N+5% fps.

dolby_vision_metadata.title: Dolby Vision Metadata Sidecar File
dolby_vision_metadata.description: Load Dolby Vision Metadata from a sidecar XML file

first_frame_index_offset.description:
Sidecar Metadata are assumed to be aligned with the first frame of the input (before any trimming is applied).
If this is not the case, you can apply an offset to the frame indexes from the metadata

_________common_definitions_input_video_.title: Ignore Overscan Override
_________common_definitions_input_video_.description:
Ignores overscan if present. If output is encoded in a format that does not contain any precise SAR information like MPEG2, this parameter will not have any effect and the expected DAR will always be applied on the full frame size. Otherwise, if output is encoded in a format with a precise SAR information like AVC or HEVC: for SD resolutions such as 720x576, 544x576, 480x576, 720x480, 544x480, 480x480, an overscan is typically encoded in input, so SAR/DAR will be adjusted to account for this overscan (e.g. expected 4:3 DAR on a 704x576 area of a 720x576 frame so SAR adjusted to 12:11). By enabling this override, overscan will be ignored in aspect ratio calculation so SAR/DAR will be interpreted on the full frame size (e.g. expected 4:3 DAR for a 720x576 frame so SAR adjusted to 16:15). Be careful, there can be a difference of behavior regarding your input: - if input is MPEG2, no precise SAR information is contained so our decoder can not deduce if overscan is present - if input is AVC or HEVC, it contains precise SAR information (thanks to 'aspect_radio_idc' VUI contained in SPS), so our decoder can deduce if overscan is encoded in input or not. | Input | Ignore Overscan | Behavior | | -------- | --------------- | ---------| | MPEG2 | False | Overscan is preserved in output and expected DAR is applied on area
without overscan (e.g. expected 4:3 DAR on a 704x576 area
of a 720x576 frame so SAR adjusted to 12:11) | | MPEG2 | True | Overscan is preserved in output and expected DAR is applied on the
full frame size(e.g. expected 4:3 DAR for a 720x576 frame
so SAR adjusted to 16:15) | | AVC/HEVC | False | Overscan is preserved in output and expected DAR is applied on area
without overscan (e.g. expected 4:3 DAR on a 704x576 area
of a 720x576 frame so SAR adjusted to 12:11) | | AVC/HEVC | True | Overscan is cropped in output (e.g. 720x576 input with
aspect_ratio_idc = 2 so SAR = 12:11 means it contains overscan, so it is
cropped to 704x576) and the resizing + expected DAR is applied on the
cropped image (e.g. 704x576 cropped input resized to 720x576 with
expected 4:3 DAR applied on the full 720x576 frame so SAR
adjusted to 16:15) |

_________common_definitions_input_video_.title: Colorimetry Parameters Overrides
_________common_definitions_input_video_.description: Colorimetry parameters can be signaled in input file. These can be overridden thanks to following parameters. Unlike overrides at track level, the values here will impact how the input signal is processed.

transfer_characteristics.title: Transfer Characteristics
transfer_characteristics.description:
Overrides source transfer characteristics.
Info carried by this field depends on the input characteristics:
  - Input containing an alternative_transfer_characteristics: current parameter will override the VUI property of the source,
that is the SDR backward compatible one. The HDR one (i.e. HLG), will sit in the alternative transfer characterists
field that can be override independently thanks to the alternative_transfer_characterists field below.
  - Input without alternative_transfer_characteristics: current parameter will still override the VUI property of the source,
but which this time represents the real transfer characteristics (i.e. HDR one)\

If any alternative_transfer_characteristics is defined, that will be the one used to perform colorimetry conversion (if any),
otherwise transfer_characteristics will be used.

policy.title: Override Video Range Policy
policy.description:
Defines how the probed `video_range` value will be changed by user configuration:
- `never`: keeps the probed value ; if nothing has been probed, keep an empty value
- `always`: always use the user-provided value

_________common_definitions_input_video_.title: Input video filters
_________common_definitions_input_video_.description:
Each field of this object must be a stream selection filter that will be use to identify tracks in asset.
To be selected an Elementary Stream from an asset must match all the selection filters.

_________common_definitions_video_codec_.title: Video Codec
_________common_definitions_video_codec_.description: Video stream encoding standard. The stream is selected if it is a video stream encoded with the provided codec. If this field is not present, the video compression standard is not taken into account for stream selection. Value _m2v_ is kept for backward compatibility.

track.title: Track
track.description: Video Track from which the thumbnails will be generated

picture_selection_mode.title: Picture Selection Mode
picture_selection_mode.description: The picture selection mode between `period` and `number`.

number.title: Number
number.description:
Desired number of thumbnails.
The selection period will depend on the duration of the transcoded content.

size.title: Size
size.description:
Size of the thumbnail.
In order to set the size you can configure:
* Both of __width__ and __height__ so the size of your thumbnails will be what you configure
* Only __width__ so the height will be automatically computed to respect the input aspect_ratio after cropping
* Only __height__ so the width will be automatically computed to respect the input aspect_ratio after cropping

paths.title: Paths
paths.description:
List of paths where the thumbnails must be generated.
Following variables can be used to define the media path template:
 * ${INDEX}: Ordered integers will be used as index of the thumbnail files
 * ${TIMESTAMP}: Timestamp will be used as index of the thumbnail files
 * ${TIMECODE}: Timecode will be used as index of the thumbnail files
If none of them appear in the path, ${INDEX} will be used by default to index your thumbnail files, unless using a mosaic.
For mosaics, these will be replaced by 0 (or 00000000 for timecode).

items.description: One path string with or without variables

format.title: Format
format.description: Format of the thumbnail. Only jpeg is supported for now.

mosaic.title: Mosaic
mosaic.description:
Mosaic settings. When defined, thumbnails will be gathered in a single picture without being resized.
If only one dimension is configured, the other will be adjusted to fit the total number of thumbnails in one mosaic.
If both dimensions are configured, either unused spaces will be left blank if there are not enough thumbnails,
or the last thumbnails will be omitted if there are too many of them. 
Generated mosaic can not exceed 2Gb so the image must contain a maximum of 1 431 655 765 pixels.
Whether it be in width or in height the number of pixels can not exceed 65 536.

vtt_file.title: Thumbnail VTT File
vtt_file.description:
Generate VTT file for thumbnail previews as used by JWPlayer.
The VTT file will be generated in the same directory of the thumbnail.
Thumbnail paths will be appended with the appropriate sprite coordinates, e.g. "thumbnails.jpg#xywh=0,0,160,90"

colorimetry.title: Colorimetry
colorimetry.description:
Colorimetry conversion processing parameters. 
Applies offsets to the different colorimetry components of the thumbnail images.

id.title: Unique Identifier

name.title: Name
name.description: The user friendly name of the probe.

priority.title: Priority
priority.description: The priority determines the processing order of this probe. Higher priorities are processed first.

inerror_state_reason.title: Error reason
inerror_state_reason.description: This field shows the reason linked to the error when the probe is in 'inerror' state.

creation_date.title: Creation date
creation_date.description: Date the probe request was submitted.

start_date.title: Start date
start_date.description: Date the probe task state changed to running.

end_date.title: End date
end_date.description: Date the probe task state changed to aborted/completed/inerror

duration.title: Duration
duration.description: Probe task execution time in seconds.

progress.title: Progress
progress.description: The progress of the probe task in percentage.

probe_state_yaml.title: State
probe_state_yaml.description: Current state of the probe task.

id.title: Unique Identifier

name.title: Name
name.description: The user friendly name of the probe.

priority.title: Priority
priority.description:
The priority determines the processing order of this probe.
Higher priority probes will be processed first.

not.title: Version
not.description:
Version of the configuration.
If you do not provide the version, the data is considered to be formatted with version 1.0.
In this case, the configuration will be updated to the latest version.

detect_nielsen_audio_watermarking.title: Nielsen Audio Watermarking Detection
detect_nielsen_audio_watermarking.description: Enable detection of the presence of Nielsen watermarking on audio streams.

detect_video_black_bars.title: Video Black Bars Detection
detect_video_black_bars.description: Enable detection of the presence of black bars on video streams.

inerror_state_reason.title: Error reason
inerror_state_reason.description: This field shows the reason linked to the error when the probe is in 'inerror' state.

creation_date.title: Creation date
creation_date.description: Date the probe request was submitted.

start_date.title: Start date
start_date.description: Date the probe task state changed to running.

end_date.title: End date
end_date.description: Date the probe task state changed to aborted/completed/inerror

duration.title: Duration
duration.description: Probe task execution time in seconds.

node.title: Node name
node.description: The K8S node on which the probe task has been processed or is being processed.

progress.title: Progress
progress.description: The progress of the probe task in percentage.

task_configuration.title: Configuration of the probe task
task_configuration.description: Internal configuration used by the probe service.

pipelines.title: Pipelines
pipelines.description: Pipelines executed during the probe task.

uri.title: Asset URL
uri.description: URL of the asset in an internet-like format such as : protocol://credential@directory/filename.ext

type.title: Asset type
type.description: This field is used to differentiate the asset type when it is not obvious from the file extension

audio_composition.title: Audio composition
audio_composition.description:
With the audio composition, you can group multiple input audio tracks into multi-channel audio tracks.
The multi-channel audio tracks consist of an array of channels number.
This is limited to mono-channel or dual mono-channel PCM inputs embedded in a container such as MXF or MOV.
A classic usecase would be to aggregate an AES3 pair from 2 PCM mono-channel tracks.

optional.title: Asset optional
optional.description:
If set to true, this field allows the job to be transcoded even if the asset does not exist or it is inaccessible. It is assumed 
that no input with mandatory mapping policy uses this asset.

oneOf.title: Asset URL
oneOf.description: Deprecated since TF 3.0. Will be removed in a future release.

kantar_server_context.title: Kantar Server Context
kantar_server_context.description:
Provides necessary options and login information for interacting with a
Kantar server.

server_url.description: URL of the Kantar server, written as "\<host\>:\<port\>"
server_url.title: Server URL

password.title: Kantar server password
password.description: Password matching the login provided. While mandatory for POST/PUT requests, it will be omitted from any response object

server_address.title: Server address
server_address.description: The address of the license server.

status.title: Validate status
status.description: The validation status on the license server.

mvhevc_encoding.title: MV-HEVC video encoding (including Dolby Vision Profile 20)

version.description:
Version of the configuration.

If you do not provide the version, the data is considered to be formatted with version 1.0.
In this case, the configuration will be updated to the latest version.

id.title: Template unique identifier
id.description: This unique identifier is automatically generated at template creation.

name.title: Template name.
name.description:
Use this template name only for template creation. You can use this template name to make reference to the 
template when another template or job needs to use this template name. As you can use this template name as 
reference, this template name must be unique (no clones).

version.title: Version
version.description:
Version of the configuration.

If you do not provide the version, the data is considered to be formatted with version 1.0.
In this case, the configuration will be updated to the latest version.

tags.title: Tags list
tags.description: You can use tags for logical grouping of templates.

protected.title: Templete protection flag.
protected.description:
If set to true, the template cannot be modified or deleted.
This flag is used to protect defautl templates.

processing_resources_profile_id.title: Processing resources profile ID
processing_resources_profile_id.description:
The identifier that targets a resources profile ID in the Values of the helm chart (global.processing.resources.profiles.[id]). 
With this identifier, you can limit resources (memory and cpu) for processing tasks by selecting a user-defined profile in the charts Helm. 
For example, you may want to control workloads for some specifics Jobs.

cores.title: Cores
cores.description: The load of the job that determines the number of cores that must be reserved to make the job run

processing_queue.title: Processing Queue
processing_queue.description:
Podscaler queue used for all the processing pods of the job.
If the queue is stuck, jobs set on other queues will still be executed.
If the queue does not exist, the job will run on the podscaler `default` queue.

processing_node_affinity.title: Processing Node Affinity
processing_node_affinity.description:
List of labels to select a node where all the processing pods will run.
For the following explanations, we assume:
- required affinities are affinities with required_for_scheduling flag
- preferred affinities are affinities without required_for_scheduling flag
A node will be selected if all required affinities are equal to one of the configured values.
Moreover if some of those nodes are also satisfying all the preferred affinities, they will be chosen in priority.
WARNING: If no node is fulfilling all the required affinities, processing pods will never run and will block the whole jobs' queue.
If only preferred affinities are configured and no node is matching them, processing pods will be assigned to any other available node.
It is forbidden to configure more than 1 required affinity.
Example:
  We have the following configuration:
  - label = "usage", values = "transcoding, processing", required_for_scheduling=true
  - label = "capacity", values = "tiny, low", required_for_scheduling=false
  We have a 3-nodes cluster:
  - Node1 with labels "usage=application, capacity=low"
  - Node2 with labels "usage=transcoding, capacity=high"
  - Node3 with labels "usage=processing, capacity=tiny"
  All of those nodes have sufficient resources to host the processing pods.
  Node1 is discarded: its labels do not match the required affinity.
  Node2 can be selected because it matches the required affinity, but the preferred affinity is not fulfilled.
  Node3 fulfills both required and preferred affinities so it will be preferred over Node2.
  Therefore Node3 will be selected to run processing pods. 
  If Node3 is lacking resources, Node2 will be selected.
  If both Node3 and Node2 are lacking resources, processing pods will stay in a pending state until resources are freed, as Node1 can not be selected.

values.title: Values
values.description: Only one of the configured values is enough to fulfill the label affinity

required_for_scheduling.title: Required for Scheduling
required_for_scheduling.description:
If true, the affinity is a 'requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution' Kubernetes node affinity rule.
If false, the affinity is ANDed in the 'preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution' Kubernetes 
conditions with others preferred affinities.

using.title: Template reference
using.description:
This field specifies which template(s) you can use. This field can contain a single template name or a list of 
multiple templates names. 
You only need this field when you create a template or a job from templates. 
If this field is a list when you use each template from the list as a layer. 
These layers are applied according to the order of appearance in the list.

distributed_transcoding.title: Distributed transcoding configuration
distributed_transcoding.description: Distributed configuration, either as a number of parallel chunks, or a chunk duration.

chunk_number.title: Chunk number
chunk_number.description: The number of parallel chunks required to perform segment transcoding.

chunk_duration.title: Chunk duration
chunk_duration.description:
The maximum length of content in seconds that can be transcoded in a single chunk. The number of
parallel chunks to perform segment transcoding is then determined by the following formula:
`chunk_number = ceil(segment_duration / chunk_duration)`
Examples:
- for a segment of 300 seconds and a "chunk_duration" of 70 seconds we will obtain 5 chunks of 60 seconds.
- for a segment of 300 seconds and a "chunk_duration" of 120 seconds we will obtain 3 chunks of 100 seconds.
- for a segment of 300 seconds and a "chunk_duration" of 200 seconds we will obtain 2 chunks of 150 seconds.

ad_splicing.title: Ad Splicing
ad_splicing.description:
Enable ad splicing. With this option, audio frames will not be aligned on video frames.
Only works with TS muxers and with multiple_audio_frames_per_pes disabled.

smart_audio_video_alignment.title: Smart audio/video alignment
smart_audio_video_alignment.description:
When enabled, ensures that audio and video tracks have the exact same durations, duplicating video frames and inserting audio silence if required.
Only works in the use case complying to the following conditions:
- single video track with a framerate multiple of 25fps, with an encoding different than Dolby Vision
- only audio tracks that are encoded in AC-3 ("dolby_digital" and "dolby_digital_plus") or MPEG Layer 2 ("m2a"), using a samplerate of 48kHz or 32kHz
- TS muxer

custom_vars.title: Custom variables
custom_vars.description:
This object contains pairs of keys/values which can be used to substitute string values inside the job.
These strings can be both keys and values. To do this, the following wildcard format ${VAR_NAME} should be used inside the string value that you want to customize.

Example:
- we can define a file name in a custom var "FILE": "MyFile" then in the asset path "uri": "smb://my_server/sources/${FILE}.ts"
- we want to define dynamic track name "tracks": { "${MY_TRACK}": {...} }

Note that we have some limitations:
- redundant substitution are not supported. ex: {"MY_VAR": "${MY_VAR}"} or {"MY_VAR": "test", "MY_VAR2": "my_${MY_VAR}"}
- we can not substitute system words (keys or structures) ex: "assets" key, "progressif" enum value.
- usage of system wildcards are rejected.

url.title: Temporary directory URL
url.description:
Specifies the URL of the directory that will be used as temporary storage area.
If specified, temporary files will be held in this directory. Otherwise, temporary files will be held in job output paths.
This parameter has several applications:
- be able to hold temporary files on a storage that is not the one used for outputs.
- be able to support FTP outputs with features that require file seek operations (outputs of type mp4/mov/mxf and distributed transcoding)
Note that FTP storages (FTP, FTPS, and SFTP) are not supported due to their file seek limitations.

force_for_all_io_operations.title: Force use of the temporary directory for all I/O operations
force_for_all_io_operations.description:
When enabled, the temporary directory is also used as the I/O working directory.
This means that:
- input assets are copied to the temporary directory before processing
- transcoding output results are stored in the temporary directory before delivery

assets.title: Assets
assets.description:
This object contains the assets that can be referenced by tracks in inputs. Names of assets object are used to
reference them. The same asset can be referenced by multiple inputs tracks.

chunking.title: Chunking configuration
chunking.description:
This object contains the chunking configuration.
If you remove this object, you disable chunking.
2 configuration modes are possible:
- The period + jitter mode: The jitter is the allowed deviation to the nominal chunk period, to accomodate for scene cuts that occur near the chunk period.
Setting a jitter implies that the chunk boundary is placed up to half the jitter before or after the theorical boundary.
If _P_ is the nominal period and _J_ the jitter, the minimum chunk duration is _P_ - _J_ and the maximum is _P_ + _J_, as the boundary is inserted at multiples of _P_ plus or minus _J_/2.
If a chunk boundary is shifted from the nominal period, the following chunks boundaries will be computed in order to make up for the delta produced.
The consequence is the chunks number will be fixed.

- The min/max mode: Each chunk have a duration between the `minimum_chunk_duration` and `maximum_chunk_duration`.
There is no constraints to have a fixed number of chunks.
The chunk boundaries are computed following the scene cuts detected in a window beginning at _B_ + _MIN_ and going to _B_ + _MAX_ where _B_ is the end boundary of the previous chunk, _MIN_ the minimum chunk duration and _MAX_ the maximum chunk duration.

_________common_definitions_output_track.description: Output Track Identifier for this stream. If this field is zero or missing a default value will be used.

specifies which definition of SMIL to comply to.
This essentially impacts how language & bitrate are represented in the manifest:
  - v1.0 uses hyphens between words (system-language / system-bitrate)
  - v2.0/v2.1/v3.0 use camelcase (systemLanguage / systemBitrate)
